class.doc: Contains animation curves for translation/rotation/scale of scene objects/skeleton bones, as well as curves for generic property animation.

ctor.doc: Creates an animation clip with no curves. After creation make sure to register some animation curves before using it.
Creates an animation clip with specified curves.

param.name: curves
param.doc: Curves to initialize the animation with.

param.name: isAdditive
param.doc: Determines does the clip contain additive curve data. This will change the behaviour how is the clip blended with other animations.

param.name: sampleRate
param.doc: If animation uses evenly spaced keyframes, number of samples per second. Not relevant if keyframes are unevenly spaced.

param.name: rootMotion
param.doc: Optional set of curves that can be used for animating the root bone. Not used by the animation system directly but is instead provided to the user for manual evaluation.

property.name: Curves
property.doc: A set of all curves stored in the animation. Returned value will not be updated if the animation clip curves are added or removed, as it is a copy of clip's internal values.

property.name: Events
property.doc: A set of all events to be triggered as the animation is playing.

property.name: RootMotion
property.doc: Returns a set of curves containing motion of the root bone. This allows the user to evaluate the root bone animation curves manually, instead of through the normal animation process. This property is only available if animation clip was imported with root motion import enabled.

property.name: HasRootMotion
property.doc: Checks if animation clip has root motion curves separate from the normal animation curves.

property.name: IsAddtive
property.doc: Checks are the curves contained within the clip additive. Additive clips are intended to be added on top of other clips.

property.name: Length
property.doc: Returns the length of the animation clip, in seconds.

property.name: SampleRate
property.doc: Number of samples per second the animation clip curves were sampled at. This value is not used by the animation clip or curves directly since unevenly spaced keyframes are supported. But it can be of value when determining the original sample rate of an imported animation or similar.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a VectorField.

field.name: CountX
field.doc: Number of entries in the vector field along the X axis.

field.name: CountY
field.doc: Number of entries in the vector field along the Y axis.

field.name: CountZ
field.doc: Number of entries in the vector field along the Z axis.

field.name: Bounds
field.doc: Spatial bounds of the vector field.

property.name: Center
property.doc: Determines the position of the box shape, relative to the component's scene object.

enum.doc: Type of mesh indices used, used for determining maximum number of vertices in a mesh.

enumentry.doc: 16-bit indices.
32-bit indices.

enum.doc: Values that represent in which order are euler angles applied when used in transformations.
Represents verbosity level at which a specific log message will be displayed.

enumentry.doc: Fatal error happened that application cannot recover from and will crash.
An error happened that will not result in an immediate crash but may cause serious problems.
Something went wrong but the application will not crash, although invalid behaviour might be observed.
An informational message will be logged, can be used for debugging and tracing.
Same as Info, but the message will only be logged to the log file and not any console output.
Messages that can provide additional information and warnings, but are too spammy to be displayed under normal circumstances.
Same as Verbose, but for even spammier messages.
Meta-type encompassing all verbosity types. Should not be used for logging directly.

class.doc: Contains information about bones required for skeletal animation.

method.doc: Returns information about a bone at the provided index.

param.name: boneIdx
param.doc: Index of the bone to retrieve information for.

returns.doc: Information about the bone at the specified index.

property.name: NumBones
property.doc: Returns the total number of bones in the skeleton.

struct.doc: Single key in a ColorGradient.

method.doc: Changes a weight of a single morph channel, determining how much of it to apply on top of the base mesh.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name of the morph channel to modify. This depends on the mesh the animation is currently animating.

param.name: weight
param.doc: Weight that determines how much of the channel to apply to the mesh, in range [0, 1].

property.name: DefaultClip
property.doc: Determines the default clip to play as soon as the component is enabled. If more control over playing clips is needed use the play(), blend() and crossFade() methods to queue clips for playback manually, and setState() method for modify their states individually.

property.name: Bounds
property.doc: Determines bounds that will be used for animation and mesh culling. Only relevant if setUseBounds() is set to true.

property.name: UseBounds
property.doc: Determines should animation bounds be used for visibility determination (culling). If false the bounds of the mesh attached to the relevant CRenderable component will be used instead.

property.name: Cull
property.doc: Enables or disables culling of the animation when out of view. Culled animation will not be evaluated.

struct.doc: A three dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
A three dimensional vector with integer coordinates.

class.doc: Script-exported API for Application.

enum.doc: Projection type to use by the camera.

enumentry.doc: Projection type where object size remains constant and parallel lines remain parallel.
Projection type that emulates human vision. Objects farther away appear smaller.

class.doc: Represents a range of color values over some parameters, similar to a curve. Internally represented as a set of keys that get interpolated between. Stores colors as 32-bit integers, and is therefor unable to represent a color range outside of [0, 1] - see ColorGradientHDR for an alternative.

method.doc: Keys that control the gradient, sorted by time from first to last. Key times should be in range [0, 1].
Returns the color key at the specified index. If out of range an empty key is returned.
Specify a "gradient" that represents a single color value.

property.name: NumKeys
property.doc: Returns the number of color keys in the gradient.

class.doc: Generates pseudo random numbers using the Xorshift128 algorithm. Suitable for high performance requirements.

ctor.doc: Initializes a new generator using the specified seed.

method.doc: Changes the seed of the generator to the specified value.
Returns a random value in range [0, std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()].
Returns a random value in range [min, max].
Returns a random value in range [0, 1].
Returns a random value in range [-1, 1].
Returns a random unit vector in three dimensions.
Returns a random unit vector in two dimensions.
Returns a random point inside a unit sphere.

Returns a random point inside the specified range in a sphere shell of unit radius, with the specified thickness, in range [0, 1]. Thickness of 0 will generate points on the sphere surface, while thickness of 1 will generate points within the entire sphere volume. Intermediate values represent the shell, which is a volume between two concentric spheres.
Returns a random point inside a unit circle.

Returns a random point inside the specified range in a circle shell of unit radius, with the specified thickness, in range [0, 1]. Thickness of 0 will generate points on the circle edge, while thickness of 1 will generate points within the entire circle surface. Intermediate values represent the shell, which is the surface between two concentric circles.
Returns a random point on a unit arc with the specified length (angle). Angle of 360 represents a circle.

Returns a random point inside the specified range in an arc shell of unit radius, with the specified length (angle) and thickness in range [0, 1]. Angle of 360 represents a circle shell. Thickness of 0 will generate points on the arc edge, while thickness of 1 will generate points on the entire arc 'slice'. Intermediate vlaues represent the shell, which is the surface between two concentric circles.
Returns a random set of Barycentric coordinates that may be used for generating random points on a triangle.

class.doc: Contains a group of resources imported from a single source file.

Represents a range of color values over some parameters, similar to a curve. Internally represented as a set of keys that get interpolated between. Capable of representing HDR colors, unlike the normal ColorGradient.

method.doc: Keys that control the gradient, sorted by time from first to last. Key times should be in range [0, 1].
Returns the color key at the specified index. If out of range an empty key is returned.
Specify a "gradient" that represents a single color value.

property.name: NumKeys
property.doc: Returns the number of color keys in the gradient.

struct.doc: Structure that is used for initializing a render window.

field.name: VideoMode
field.doc: Output monitor, frame buffer resize and refresh rate.

field.name: Fullscreen
field.doc: Should the window be opened in fullscreen mode.

field.name: Vsync
field.doc: Should the window wait for vertical sync before swapping buffers.

field.name: VsyncInterval
field.doc: Determines how many vsync intervals occur per frame. FPS = refreshRate/interval. Usually 1 when vsync active.

field.name: Hidden
field.doc: Should the window be hidden initially.

field.name: DepthBuffer
field.doc: Should the window be created with a depth/stencil buffer.

field.name: MultisampleCount
field.doc: If higher than 1, texture containing multiple samples per pixel is created.

field.name: MultisampleHint
field.doc: Hint about what kind of multisampling to use. Render system specific.

field.name: Gamma
field.doc: Should the written color pixels be gamma corrected before write.

field.name: Left
field.doc: Window origin on X axis in pixels. -1 == screen center. Relative to monitor provided in videoMode.

field.name: Top
field.doc: Window origin on Y axis in pixels. -1 == screen center. Relative to monitor provided in videoMode.

field.name: Title
field.doc: Title of the window.

field.name: ShowTitleBar
field.doc: Determines if the title-bar should be shown or not.

field.name: ShowBorder
field.doc: Determines if the window border should be shown or not.

field.name: AllowResize
field.doc: Determines if the user can resize the window by dragging on the window edges.

field.name: ToolWindow
field.doc: Tool windows have no task bar entry and always remain on top of their parent window.

field.name: Modal
field.doc: When a modal window is open all other windows will be locked until modal window is closed.

field.name: HideUntilSwap
field.doc: Window will be created as hidden and only be shown when the first framebuffer swap happens.

enum.doc: Determines type of distribution used by distribution properties.

enumentry.doc: The distribution is a costant value.
The distribution is a random value in a specified constant range.
The distribution is a time-varying value.
The distribution is a random value in a specified time-varying range.

class.doc: Animation spline represented by a set of keyframes, each representing an endpoint of a cubic hermite curve. The spline can be evaluated at any time, and uses caching to speed up multiple sequential evaluations.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

param.name: keyframes
param.doc: Keyframes to initialize the curve with. They must be sorted by time.

method.doc: Evaluate the animation curve at the specified time. If evaluating multiple values in a sequential order consider using the cached version of evaluate() for better performance.

param.name: time
param.doc: %Time to evaluate the curve at.

param.name: loop
param.doc: If true the curve will loop when it goes past the end or beggining. Otherwise the curve value will be clamped.

returns.doc: Interpolated value from the curve at provided time.

property.name: KeyFrames
property.doc: Returns a list of all keyframes in the curve.

class.doc: Animation spline represented by a set of keyframes, each representing an endpoint of a cubic hermite curve. The spline can be evaluated at any time, and uses caching to speed up multiple sequential evaluations.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

param.name: keyframes
param.doc: Keyframes to initialize the curve with. They must be sorted by time.

method.doc: Evaluate the animation curve at the specified time. If evaluating multiple values in a sequential order consider using the cached version of evaluate() for better performance.

param.name: time
param.doc: %Time to evaluate the curve at.

param.name: loop
param.doc: If true the curve will loop when it goes past the end or beggining. Otherwise the curve value will be clamped.

returns.doc: Interpolated value from the curve at provided time.

property.name: KeyFrames
property.doc: Returns a list of all keyframes in the curve.

class.doc: Animation spline represented by a set of keyframes, each representing an endpoint of a cubic hermite curve. The spline can be evaluated at any time, and uses caching to speed up multiple sequential evaluations.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

param.name: keyframes
param.doc: Keyframes to initialize the curve with. They must be sorted by time.

method.doc: Evaluate the animation curve at the specified time. If evaluating multiple values in a sequential order consider using the cached version of evaluate() for better performance.

param.name: time
param.doc: %Time to evaluate the curve at.

param.name: loop
param.doc: If true the curve will loop when it goes past the end or beggining. Otherwise the curve value will be clamped.

returns.doc: Interpolated value from the curve at provided time.

property.name: KeyFrames
property.doc: Returns a list of all keyframes in the curve.

class.doc: Animation spline represented by a set of keyframes, each representing an endpoint of a cubic hermite curve. The spline can be evaluated at any time, and uses caching to speed up multiple sequential evaluations.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

param.name: keyframes
param.doc: Keyframes to initialize the curve with. They must be sorted by time.

method.doc: Evaluate the animation curve at the specified time. If evaluating multiple values in a sequential order consider using the cached version of evaluate() for better performance.

param.name: time
param.doc: %Time to evaluate the curve at.

param.name: loop
param.doc: If true the curve will loop when it goes past the end or beggining. Otherwise the curve value will be clamped.

returns.doc: Interpolated value from the curve at provided time.

property.name: KeyFrames
property.doc: Returns a list of all keyframes in the curve.

class.doc: Animation spline represented by a set of keyframes, each representing an endpoint of a cubic hermite curve. The spline can be evaluated at any time, and uses caching to speed up multiple sequential evaluations.

ctor.doc: Creates a new animation curve.

param.name: keyframes
param.doc: Keyframes to initialize the curve with. They must be sorted by time.

method.doc: Evaluate the animation curve at the specified time. If evaluating multiple values in a sequential order consider using the cached version of evaluate() for better performance.

param.name: time
param.doc: %Time to evaluate the curve at.

param.name: loop
param.doc: If true the curve will loop when it goes past the end or beggining. Otherwise the curve value will be clamped.

returns.doc: Interpolated value from the curve at provided time.

property.name: KeyFrames
property.doc: Returns a list of all keyframes in the curve.

struct.doc: Defines a 1D blend where multiple animation clips are blended between each other using linear interpolation.
A two dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
A two dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
Video mode contains information about how a render window presents its information to an output device like a monitor.

ctor.doc: Creates a new video mode.

param.name: width
param.doc: Width of the frame buffer in pixels.

param.name: height
param.doc: Height of the frame buffer in pixels.

param.name: refreshRate
param.doc: How often should the output device refresh the output image in hertz.

param.name: outputIdx
param.doc: Output index of the output device. Normally this means output monitor. 0th index always represents the primary device while order of others is undefined.

field.name: Width
field.doc: Width of the front/back buffer in pixels.

field.name: Height
field.doc: Height of the front/back buffer in pixels.

field.name: RefreshRate
field.doc: Refresh rate in hertz.

field.name: OutputIdx
field.doc: Index of the parent video output.

field.name: IsCustom
field.doc: Determines was video mode user created or provided by the API/OS. API/OS created video modes can contain additional information that allows the video mode to be used more accurately and you should use them when possible.

enum.doc: Type of parameters that can be defined by a shader.

struct.doc: A four dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
A four dimensional vector with integer coordinates.
Information describing a ParticleEmitterCircleShape.

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of the circle.

field.name: Thickness
field.doc: Proportion of the surface that can emit particles. Thickness of 0 results in particles being emitted only from the edge of the circle, while thickness of 1 results in particles being emitted from the entire surface. In-between values will use a part of the surface.

field.name: Arc
field.doc: Angular portion of the cone from which to emit particles from, in degrees.

field.name: Mode
field.doc: Determines how will particle positions on the shape be generated.

enum.doc: Types of available chromatic aberration effects.

enumentry.doc: Simple chromatic aberration effect that is fast to execute.
More complex chromatic aberration effect that takes longer to execute but may yield more visually pleasing results than the simple variant.

class.doc: String handle. Provides a wrapper around an Unicode string, primarily for localization purposes. Actual value for this string is looked up in a global string table based on the provided identifier string and currently active language. If such value doesn't exist then the identifier is used as is. Use {0}, {1}, etc. in the string value for values that might change dynamically.

ctor.doc: Creates a new localized string with the specified identifier. If the identifier doesn't previously exist in the string table, identifier value will also be used for initializing the default language version of the string.

param.name: identifier
param.doc: String you can use for later referencing the localized string.

param.name: stringTableId
param.doc: Unique identifier of the string table to retrieve the string from.

ctor.doc: Creates a new localized string with the specified identifier and sets the default language version of the string. If a string with that identifier already exists default language string will be updated.

param.name: identifier
param.doc: String you can use for later referencing the localized string.

param.name: defaultString
param.doc: Default string to assign to the specified identifier. Language to which it will be assigned depends on the StringTable::DEFAULT_LANGUAGE value.

param.name: stringTableId
param.doc: Unique identifier of the string table to retrieve the string from.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty localized string.

param.name: stringTableId
param.doc: Unique identifier of the string table to retrieve the string from.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty localized string.

method.doc: Sets a value of a string parameter. Parameters are specified as bracketed values within the string itself (for example {0}, {1}) etc. Use ^ as an escape character.

enum.doc: Describes operation that will be used for rendering a certain set of vertices.

enumentry.doc: Each vertex represents a point.
Each sequential pair of vertices represent a line.
Each vertex (except the first) forms a line with the previous vertex.
Each sequential 3-tuple of vertices represent a triangle.
Each vertex (except the first two) form a triangle with the previous two vertices.
Each vertex (except the first two) form a triangle with the first vertex and previous vertex.

class.doc: Primary class for holding geometry. Stores data in the form of vertex buffers and optionally an index buffer, which may be bound to the pipeline for drawing. May contain multiple sub-meshes.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh with enough space to hold the a number of primitives using the specified layout. All indices will be part of a single sub-mesh.

param.name: numVertices
param.doc: Number of vertices in the mesh.

param.name: numIndices
param.doc: Number of indices in the mesh. Must be a multiple of primitive size as specified by provided topology.

param.name: topology
param.doc: Determines how should the provided indices be interpreted by the pipeline. Default option is a triangle list, where three indices represent a single triangle.

param.name: usage
param.doc: Optimizes performance depending on planned usage of the mesh.

param.name: vertex
param.doc: Controls how are vertices organized in the vertex buffer and what data they contain.

param.name: index
param.doc: Size of indices, use smaller size for better performance, however be careful not to go over the number of vertices limited by the data type size.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh with enough space to hold the a number of primitives using the specified layout. Indices can be referenced by multiple sub-meshes.

param.name: numVertices
param.doc: Number of vertices in the mesh.

param.name: numIndices
param.doc: Number of indices in the mesh. Must be a multiple of primitive size as specified by provided topology.

param.name: subMeshes
param.doc: Defines how are indices separated into sub-meshes, and how are those sub-meshes rendered. Sub-meshes may be rendered independently, each with a different material.

param.name: usage
param.doc: Optimizes performance depending on planned usage of the mesh.

param.name: vertex
param.doc: Controls how are vertices organized in the vertex buffer and what data they contain.

param.name: index
param.doc: Size of indices, use smaller size for better performance, however be careful not to go over the number of vertices limited by the data type size.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh from an existing mesh data. Created mesh will match the vertex and index buffers described by the mesh data exactly. Mesh will have no sub-meshes.

param.name: data
param.doc: Vertex and index data to initialize the mesh with.

param.name: topology
param.doc: Determines how should the provided indices be interpreted by the pipeline. Default option is a triangle list, where three indices represent a single triangle.

param.name: usage
param.doc: Optimizes performance depending on planned usage of the mesh.

ctor.doc: Creates a new mesh with enough space to hold the a number of primitives using the specified layout. Indices can be referenced by multiple sub-meshes.

param.name: data
param.doc: Vertex and index data to initialize the mesh with.

param.name: subMeshes
param.doc: Defines how are indices separated into sub-meshes, and how are those sub-meshes rendered. Sub-meshes may be rendered independently, each with a different material.

param.name: usage
param.doc: Optimizes performance depending on planned usage of the mesh.

property.name: Skeleton
property.doc: Gets the skeleton required for animation of this mesh, if any is available.

property.name: MorphShapes
property.doc: Returns an object containing all shapes used for morph animation, if any are available.

property.name: SubMeshes
property.doc: Returns all sub-meshes contained in the mesh.

property.name: SubMeshCount
property.doc: Returns the number of sub-meshes contained in this mesh.

property.name: MeshData
property.doc: Accesses the vertex and index data of the mesh. If reading, mesh must have been created with the MeshUsage::CPUCached flag. If writing the caller must ensure the data matches mesh's vertex/index counts, vertex layout and index format.

class.doc: Updates properties of all active particles in a particle system in some way.

enum.doc: Flags that determine which portion of the viewport to clear.

class.doc: Render target specialization that allows you to render into one or multiple textures. Such textures can then be used in other operations as GPU program input.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture.

param.name: format
param.doc: Pixel format of the texture. Format must be a valid uncompressed color format.

param.name: width
param.doc: Width of the texture in pixels.

param.name: height
param.doc: Height of the texture in pixels.

param.name: numSamples
param.doc: Number of samples contained per pixel.

param.name: gammaCorrection
param.doc: Determines should the pixels written on the texture be gamma corrected.

param.name: createDepth
param.doc: Should the render texture also contain a depth/stencil buffer.

param.name: depthStencilFormat
param.doc: Format of the depth/stencil buffer, if is enabled. Format must be a valid depth/stencil format.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using an existing color texture, and no depth-stencil texture.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture to render color data to.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using existing textures as render destinations.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture to render color data to.

param.name: depthStencilSurface
param.doc: Optional depth/stencil texture to render depth/stencil data to.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using one or multiple color textures and no depth-stencil texture.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture(s) to render color data to.

ctor.doc: Creates a new 2D render texture using one or multiple color textures and a depth/stencil texture.

param.name: colorSurface
param.doc: Color texture(s) to render color data to.

param.name: depthStencilSurface
param.doc: Optional depth/stencil texture to render depth/stencil data to.

property.name: ColorSurface
property.doc: Returns the primary color surface that contains rendered color data.

property.name: ColorSurfaces
property.doc: Returns all the color surfaces.

property.name: DepthStencilSurface
property.doc: Returns the depth/stencil surface that contains rendered depth and stencil data.

enum.doc: Available texture types.

enumentry.doc: One dimensional texture. Just a row of pixels.
Two dimensional texture.
Three dimensional texture.
Texture consisting out of six 2D textures describing an inside of a cube. Allows special sampling.

enum.doc: Valid types of a mesh used for physics.

enumentry.doc: A regular triangle mesh. Mesh can be of arbitrary size but cannot be used for triggers and non-kinematic objects. Incurrs a significantly larger performance impact than convex meshes.
Mesh representing a convex shape. Mesh will not have more than 256 vertices. Incurrs a significantly lower performance impact than triangle meshes.

enum.doc: Type of force that can be applied to a rigidbody at an arbitrary point.

enumentry.doc: Value applied is a force.
Value applied is an impulse (a direct change in its linear or angular momentum).

enum.doc: Determines the type of the source image for generating cubemaps.

enumentry.doc: Source is a single image that will be replicated on all cubemap faces.
Source is a list of 6 images, either sequentially next to each other or in a cross format. The system will automatically guess the layout and orientation based on the aspect ratio.
Source is a single spherical panoramic image.
Source is a single cylindrical panoramic image.

class.doc: Base class for creating import options from. Import options are specific for each importer and control how is data imported.
Settings that control how is a VectorPath drawn.

Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a surface of a static (non-animated) mesh. Particles can be emitted from mesh vertices, edges or triangles. If information about normals exists, particles will also inherit the normals.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter static mesh shape.
Creates a new particle emitter static mesh shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Represents a 3D region of pixels used for referencing pixel data.

class.doc: Settings that control the bloom effect. Bloom adds an extra highlight to bright areas of the scene.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the bloom effect.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Quality of the bloom effect. Higher values will use higher resolution texture for calculating bloom, at the cost of lower performance. Valid range is [0, 3], default is 2.

property.name: Threshold
property.doc: Determines the minimal threshold of pixel luminance to be included in the bloom calculations. Any pixel with luminance below this value will be ignored for the purposes of bloom. The value represents luminance after it is scaled by exposure. Set to zero or negative to disable the threshold and include all pixels in the calculations.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Determines the intensity of the bloom effect. Ideally should be in [0, 4] range but higher values are allowed.

property.name: Tint
property.doc: Tint color to apply to the bloom highlight. A pure white means the bloom inherits the underlying scene color.

property.name: FilterSize
property.doc: Determines the percent of the texture to account for when filtering for bloom. Larger values will include farther away pixels.

enum.doc: Names of individual components of a vector.
Names of individual components of a color.

class.doc: Helper class for dealing with animations, animation clips and curves.

method.doc: Converts a curve in euler angles (in degrees) into a curve using quaternions.
Converts a curve in quaternions into a curve using euler angles (in degrees).
Splits a Vector3 curve into three individual curves, one for each component.
Combines three single component curves into a Vector3 curve.
Splits a Vector2 curve into two individual curves, one for each component.
Combines two single component curves into a Vector2 curve.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit between two angles.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty limit.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower angle of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper angle of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower angle of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper angle of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Lower
field.doc: Lower angle of the limit. Must be less than #upper.

field.name: Upper
field.doc: Upper angle of the limit. Must be less than #lower.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

enum.doc: Determines how an animation clip behaves when it reaches the end.

enumentry.doc: Loop around to the beginning/end when the last/first frame is reached.
Clamp to end/beginning, keeping the last/first frame active.

struct.doc: Contains internal information about a single bone in a Skeleton.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Unique name of the bone.

field.name: Parent
field.doc: Index of the parent bone (within the relevant Skeleton object). -1 if root bone.

field.name: InvBindPose
field.doc: Inverse transform of the pose the skeleton was initially created in.

enum.doc: Type of GUI element options.
Identifiers representing a range of values.

Controls what kind of mobility restrictions a scene object has. This is used primarily as a performance hint to other systems. Generally the more restricted the mobility the higher performance can be achieved.

enumentry.doc: Scene object can be moved and has no mobility restrictions.
Scene object isn't allowed to be moved but is allowed to be visually changed in other ways (e.g. changing the displayed mesh or light intensity (depends on attached components).
Scene object isn't allowed to be moved nor is it allowed to be visually changed. Object must be fully static.

class.doc: Contains a set of animation curves used for moving and rotating the root bone.

property.name: Position
property.doc: Animation curve representing the movement of the root bone.

property.name: Rotation
property.doc: Animation curve representing the rotation of the root bone.

struct.doc: References a subset of surfaces within a texture.

field.name: MipLevel
field.doc: First mip level to reference.

field.name: MipLevelCount
field.doc: Number of mip levels to reference. Must be greater than zero.

field.name: Face
field.doc: First face to reference. Face can represent a single cubemap face, or a single array entry in a texture array. If cubemaps are laid out in a texture array then every six sequential faces represent a single array entry.

field.name: FaceCount
field.doc: Number of faces to reference, if the texture has more than one.

field.name: IsBoundAs2DArray
field.doc: Forces a cubemap or a 3D texture to be bound as a 2D texture array.

class.doc: Contains a set of morph channels used for morph target animation. Each morph channel contains one or multiple shapes which are blended together depending on frame animation. Each channel is then additively blended together depending on some weight.

property.name: Channels
property.doc: Returns a list of all morph channels in the morph animation.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a surface of a skinned (animated) mesh. Particles can be emitted from mesh vertices, edges or triangles. If information about normals exists, particles will also inherit the normals.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter skinned mesh shape.
Creates a new particle emitter skinned mesh shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

enum.doc: Type of scaling modes for GUI images.

enumentry.doc: Image will stretch non-uniformly in all dimensions in order to cover the assigned area fully.
Image will scale uniformly until one dimension is aligned with the assigned area. Remaining dimension might have empty space.
Image will scale uniformly until both dimensions are larger or aligned with the assigned area. Remaining dimension might be cropped.
Image will keep its original size, but will repeat in order to fill the assigned area.

enum.doc: Determines how are particles represented on the screen.

enumentry.doc: Particle is represented using a 2D quad.
Particle is represented using a 3D mesh.

struct.doc: Rectangle represented in the form of offsets from some parent rectangle.
Contains information about a currently playing animation clip.

field.name: Layer
field.doc: Layer the clip is playing on. Multiple clips can be played simulatenously on different layers.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Current time the animation is playing from.

field.name: Speed
field.doc: Speed at which the animation is playing.

field.name: Weight
field.doc: Determines how much of an influence does the clip have on the final pose.

field.name: WrapMode
field.doc: Determines what happens to other animation clips when a new clip starts playing.

field.name: Stopped
field.doc: Determines should the time be advanced automatically. Certain type of animation clips don't involve playback (e.g. for blending where animation weight controls the animation).

struct.doc: Represents an animation clip used in 1D blending. Each clip has a position on the number line.
Specific texture and text color used in a particular GUI element style.
Defines a 2D blend where two animation clips are blended between each other using bilinear interpolation.
Animation keyframe, represented as an endpoint of a cubic hermite spline.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Value of the key.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Position of the key along the animation spline.

struct.doc: Animation keyframe, represented as an endpoint of a cubic hermite spline.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Value of the key.

field.name: InTangent
field.doc: Input tangent (going from the previous key to this one) of the key.

field.name: OutTangent
field.doc: Output tangent (going from this key to next one) of the key.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Position of the key along the animation spline.

struct.doc: Animation keyframe, represented as an endpoint of a cubic hermite spline.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Value of the key.

field.name: InTangent
field.doc: Input tangent (going from the previous key to this one) of the key.

field.name: OutTangent
field.doc: Output tangent (going from this key to next one) of the key.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Position of the key along the animation spline.

struct.doc: Animation keyframe, represented as an endpoint of a cubic hermite spline.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Value of the key.

field.name: InTangent
field.doc: Input tangent (going from the previous key to this one) of the key.

field.name: OutTangent
field.doc: Output tangent (going from this key to next one) of the key.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Position of the key along the animation spline.

struct.doc: Animation keyframe, represented as an endpoint of a cubic hermite spline.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Value of the key.

field.name: InTangent
field.doc: Input tangent (going from the previous key to this one) of the key.

field.name: OutTangent
field.doc: Output tangent (going from this key to next one) of the key.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Position of the key along the animation spline.

class.doc: Represents a three dimensional field of vectors. It is represented by spatial bounds which are split into a grid of values with user-defined density, where each grid cell is assigned a vector.

ctor.doc: Creates a new vector field.

param.name: desc
param.doc: Description of the vector field to create.

param.name: values
param.doc: Values to assign to the vector field. Number of entries must match countX * countY * countZ.

enum.doc: Flags that describe an animation curve.

enumentry.doc: If enabled, the curve was imported from an external file and not created within the engine. This will affect how are animation results applied to scene objects (with imported animations it is assumed the curve is animating bones and with in-engine curves it is assumed the curve is animating scene objects).
Signifies the curve is used to animate between different frames within a morph channel. In range [0, 1].
Signifies the curve is used to adjust the weight of a morph channel. In range [0, 1].

struct.doc: An animation curve and its name.

ctor.doc: Constructs a new named animation curve.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name of the curve.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve containing the animation data.

ctor.doc: Constructs a new named animation curve.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name of the curve.

param.name: flags
param.doc: Flags that describe the animation curve.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve containing the animation data.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Name of the curve.

field.name: Flags
field.doc: Flags that describe the animation curve.

field.name: Curve
field.doc: Actual curve containing animation data.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name of the curve.
