class.doc: A set of animation curves representing translation/rotation/scale and generic animation.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for animating position.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This name will be used mapping the curve to the relevant bone in a skeleton, if any.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for animating rotation.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This name will be used mapping the curve to the relevant bone in a skeleton, if any.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for animating scale.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This name will be used mapping the curve to the relevant bone in a skeleton, if any.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Registers a new curve used for generic animation.

param.name: name
param.doc: Unique name of the curve. This can be used for retrieving the value of the curve from animation.

param.name: curve
param.doc: Curve to add to the clip.

method.doc: Removes an existing curve from the clip.
Removes an existing curve from the clip.
Removes an existing curve from the clip.
Removes an existing curve from the clip.

property.name: Position
property.doc: Curves for animating scene object's position.

property.name: Rotation
property.doc: Curves for animating scene object's rotation.

property.name: Scale
property.doc: Curves for animating scene object's scale.

property.name: Generic
property.doc: Curves for animating generic component properties.

class.doc: Audio clip stores audio data in a compressed or uncompressed format. Clips can be provided to audio sources or other audio methods to be played.

property.name: BitDepth
property.doc: Returns the size of a single sample, in bits.

property.name: SampleRate
property.doc: Returns how many samples per second is the audio encoded in.

property.name: NumChannels
property.doc: Returns the number of channels provided by the clip.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Returns in which format is audio data stored in.

property.name: ReadMode
property.doc: Returns how is the audio data read/decoded.

property.name: Duration
property.doc: Returns the length of the audio clip, in seconds.

property.name: NumSamples
property.doc: Returns the total number of samples in the clip (includes all channels).

property.name: Is3D
property.doc: Determines will the clip be played a spatial 3D sound, or as a normal sound (for example music).

struct.doc: Event that is triggered when animation reaches a certain point.

ctor.doc: Constructs a new animation event.

param.name: name
param.doc: Name used to identify the event when triggered.

param.name: time
param.doc: Time at which to trigger the event, in seconds.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Name used to identify the event when triggered.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Time at which to trigger the event, in seconds.

struct.doc: Identifier for a device that can be used for playing audio.

enum.doc: Type of tangent on a keyframe in an animation curve.
Flags that are used for describing how are tangents calculated for a specific keyframe in an animation curve. Modes for "in" and "out" tangents can be combined.

enumentry.doc: Both tangents are calculated automatically based on the two surrounding keyframes.
Left tangent is calculated automatically based on the two surrounding keyframes.
Left tangent is manually adjusted by the user.
Tangent is calculated automatically based on the next keyframe.
Tangent is infinite, ensuring there is a instantaneus jump between previous and current keyframe value.
Tangent is calculated automatically based on the previous keyframe.
Right tangents are calculated automatically based on the two surrounding keyframes.
Right tangent is manually adjusted by the user.
Tangent is infinite, ensuring there is a instantaneus jump between current and next keyframe value.
Both tangents are manually adjusted by the user.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleSize object.

field.name: Size
field.doc: Determines the uniform size of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime. Only used if 3D size is disabled.

field.name: Size3D
field.doc: Determines the non-uniform size of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime. Only used if 3D size is enabled.

field.name: Use3DSize
field.doc: Determines should the size be evaluated uniformly for all dimensions, or evaluate each dimension with its own distribution.

struct.doc: Structure containing a reference to a keyframe tangent, as a keyframe reference and type of the tangent.

class.doc: Name and weight of a single shape in a morph target animation. Each shape internally represents a set of vertices that describe the morph shape.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Returns the name of the shape.

property.name: Weight
property.doc: Returns the weight of the shape, determining how are different shapes within a channel blended.

class.doc: A collection of morph shapes that are sequentially blended together. Each shape has a weight in range [0, 1] which determines at what point is that shape blended. As the channel percent moves from 0 to 1, different shapes will be blended with those before or after them, depending on their weight.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Returns the unique name of the channel.

property.name: Shapes
property.doc: Returns all morph shapes within this channel, in order from lowest to highest.

enum.doc: Valid states in which AudioSource can be in.

enumentry.doc: Source is currently playing.
Source is currently paused (play will resume from paused point).
Source is currently stopped (play will resume from start).

class.doc: Provides global functionality relating to sounds and music.

property.name: Volume
property.doc: Determines global audio volume. In range [0, 1].

property.name: Paused
property.doc: Determines if audio reproduction is paused globally.

property.name: ActiveDevice
property.doc: Determines the device on which is the audio played back on.

property.name: DefaultDevice
property.doc: Returns the default audio device identifier.

property.name: AllDevices
property.doc: Returns a list of all available audio devices.

enum.doc: Valid internal engine audio formats.

enumentry.doc: Pulse code modulation audio ("raw" uncompressed audio).
Vorbis compressed audio.

enum.doc: Modes that determine how and when is audio data read.

enumentry.doc: Entire audio clip will be loaded and decompressed. Uses most memory but has lowest CPU impact.
Entire audio clip will be loaded, but will be decompressed while playing. Uses medium amount of memory and has the highest CPU impact.

Audio will be slowly streamed from the disk, and decompressed if needed. Uses very little memory, and has either low or high CPU impact depending if the audio is in a compressed format. Since data is streamed from the disk, read speeds could also be a bottleneck.

class.doc: Settings that control temporal anti-aliasing.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables temporal anti-aliasing.

property.name: JitteredPositionCount
property.doc: Number of different jittered positions to use. Each frame will use one position and subsequent frames will use subsequent positions until this number of reached, at which point the positions start getting re-used from the start.

property.name: Sharpness
property.doc: Determines the distance between temporal AA samples. Larger values result in a sharper image.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how an audio clip is imported.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are audio clips imported.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Audio format to import the audio clip as.

property.name: ReadMode
property.doc: Determines how is audio data loaded into memory.

property.name: Is3D
property.doc: Determines should the clip be played as spatial (3D) audio or as normal audio. 3D clips will be converted to mono on import.

property.name: BitDepth
property.doc: Size of a single sample in bits. The clip will be converted to this bit depth on import.

struct.doc: Information about a single page containing font bitmaps.

enum.doc: Pixel formats usable by images, textures and render surfaces.

enumentry.doc: 8-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
DXT3/BC2 format containing RGB with explicit alpha. 8 bits per pixel.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
DXT1/BC1 format containing opaque RGB or 1-bit alpha RGB. 4 bits per pixel.
DXT5/BC2 format containing RGB with explicit alpha. 8 bits per pixel. Better alpha gradients than BC2.
One channel compressed format. 4 bits per pixel.
Two channel compressed format. 8 bits per pixel.
Format storing RGB in half (16-bit) floating point format usable for HDR. 8 bits per pixel.
Format storing RGB with optional alpha channel. Similar to BC1/BC2/BC3 formats but with higher quality and higher decompress overhead. 8 bits per pixel.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed float.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed float.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed float.
32-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed float.
32-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed float.
32-bit 3-channel pixel format, signed float.
32-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed float.
Depth stencil format, 32bit depth, 8bit stencil + 24 unused. Depth stored as signed float.
Depth stencil fomrat, 24bit depth + 8bit stencil. Depth stored as unsigned normalized.
Depth format, 32bits. Signed float.
Depth format, 16bits. Unsigned normalized.
8-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed integer.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed integer.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed integer.
8-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
8-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
8-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
8-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed integer.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed integer.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed integer.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
32-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed integer.
32-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed integer.
32-bit 3-channel pixel format, signed integer.
32-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed integer.
32-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
32-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
32-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
32-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned integer.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, signed normalized.
16-bit 1-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
16-bit 2-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
16-bit 3-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.
16-bit 4-channel pixel format, unsigned normalized.

class.doc: A buffer describing a volume (3D), image (2D) or line (1D) of pixels in memory. Pixels are stored as a succession of "depth" slices, each containing "height" rows of "width" pixels.

method.doc: Returns a pixel at the specified location in the buffer.

param.name: x
param.doc: X coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: y
param.doc: Y coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: z
param.doc: Z coordinate of the pixel.

returns.doc: Value of the pixel, or undefined value if coordinates are out of range.

method.doc: Sets a pixel at the specified location in the buffer.

param.name: value
param.doc: Color of the pixel to set.

param.name: x
param.doc: X coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: y
param.doc: Y coordinate of the pixel.

param.name: z
param.doc: Z coordinate of the pixel.

method.doc: Returns values of all pixels.

returns.doc: All pixels in the buffer ordered consecutively. Pixels are stored as a succession of "depth" slices, each containing "height" rows of "width" pixels.

method.doc: Sets all pixels in the buffer.Caller must ensure that number of pixels match the extends of the buffer.

param.name: value
param.doc: All pixels in the buffer ordered consecutively. Pixels are stored as a succession of "depth" slices, each containing "height" rows of "width" pixels.

method.doc: Returns all pixels in the buffer as raw bytes.

returns.doc: Raw pixel bytes. It is up to the caller to interpret the pixel format and account for potential row and slice pitch values.

method.doc: Sets all pixels in the buffer as raw bytes.

param.name: value
param.doc: Raw pixel bytes. It is up to the caller to interpret the pixel format and account for potential row and slice pitch values.

property.name: RawRowPitch
property.doc: Returns the number of bytes that offsets one row from another. This can be exact number of bytes required to hold "width" pixel, but doesn't have to be as some buffers require padding.

property.name: RawSlicePitch
property.doc: Returns the number of bytes that offsets one depth slice from another. This can be exact number of bytes required to hold "width * height" pixels, but doesn't have to be as some buffers require padding.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Returns the pixel format used by the internal buffer for storing the pixels.

property.name: Extents
property.doc: Returns extents of the pixel volume this object is capable of holding.

property.name: RawIsConsecutive
property.doc: Return whether this buffer is laid out consecutive in memory (meaning the pitches are equal to the dimensions).

property.name: RawSize
property.doc: Return the size (in bytes) of the buffer this image requires.

class.doc: Viewport determines to which RenderTarget should rendering be performed. It allows you to render to a sub-region of the target by specifying the area rectangle, and allows you to set up color/depth/stencil clear values for that specific region.

ctor.doc: Creates a new viewport.

property.name: Target
property.doc: Determines the render target the viewport is associated with.

property.name: Area
property.doc: Determines the area that the viewport covers. Coordinates are in normalized [0, 1] range.

property.name: PixelArea
property.doc: Returns the area of the render target covered by the viewport, in pixels.

property.name: ClearFlags
property.doc: Determines which portions of the render target should be cleared before rendering to this viewport is performed.

property.name: ClearColor
property.doc: Determines the color to clear the viewport to before rendering, if color clear is enabled.

property.name: ClearDepth
property.doc: Determines the value to clear the depth buffer to before rendering, if depth clear is enabled.

property.name: ClearStencil
property.doc: Determines the value to clear the stencil buffer to before rendering, if stencil clear is enabled.

class.doc: Render target is a frame buffer or a texture that the render system renders the scene to.
Settings that control the motion blur effect.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the motion blur effect.

property.name: Domain
property.doc: Determines which parts of the scene will trigger motion blur.

property.name: Filter
property.doc: Type of filter to use when filtering samples contributing to a blurred pixel.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Determines the number of samples to take during motion blur filtering. Increasing this value will yield higher quality blur at the cost of the performance.

property.name: MaximumRadius
property.doc: Determines the maximum radius over which the blur samples are allowed to be taken, in percent of the screen width (e.g. with 1% radius, on 1920x1028 resolution the maximum radius in pixels will be 1920 * 0.01 = 20px). This clamps the maximum velocity that can affect the blur, as higher velocities require higher radius. Very high values can adversely affect performance as cache accesses become more random.

struct.doc: Structure containing parameters for starting the application.

field.name: RenderApi
field.doc: Name of the render system plugin to use.

field.name: Renderer
field.doc: Name of the renderer plugin to use.

field.name: Physics
field.doc: Name of physics plugin to use.

field.name: Audio
field.doc: Name of the audio plugin to use.

field.name: Input
field.doc: Name of the input plugin to use.

field.name: PhysicsCooking
field.doc: True if physics cooking library should be loaded. Cooking is useful for creating collision meshes during development type, but might be unnecessary in the final application. When turned off you can save on space by not shipping the cooking library.

field.name: AsyncAnimation
field.doc: True if animation should be evaluated at the same time while rendering is happening. This introduces a one frame delay to all animations but can result in better performance. If false the animation will be forced to finish evaluating before rendering starts, ensuring up-to-date frame but potentially blocking the rendering thread from moving forward until the animation finishes.

field.name: PrimaryWindowDesc
field.doc: Describes the window to create during start-up.

field.name: Importers
field.doc: A list of importer plugins to load.

property.name: PlayOnStart
property.doc: Sets whether playback should start as soon as the component is enabled.

class.doc: Component that maps animation for specific bone also be applied to the SceneObject this component is attached to. The component will attach to the first found parent Animation component.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Determines the name of the bone the component is referencing.

struct.doc: Contains per-vertex bone weights and indexes used for skinning, for up to four bones.
Information about a single contact point during physics collision.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact point in world space.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Normal pointing from the second shape to the first shape.

field.name: Impulse
field.doc: Impulse applied to the objects to keep them from penetrating. Divide by simulation step to get the force.

field.name: Separation
field.doc: Determines how far are the objects. Negative value denotes penetration.

class.doc: Applies gravity to the particles.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle gravity evolver.
Creates a new particle gravity evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

enum.doc: Light type that determines how is light information parsed by the renderer and other systems.

struct.doc: Information about a collision between two physics objects.

field.name: Collider
field.doc: Components of the colliders that have collided.

field.name: ContactPoints
field.doc: Information about all the contact points for the hit.

class.doc: Settings that control the screen-space lens flare effect.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the lens flare effect.

property.name: DownsampleCount
property.doc: Determines how many times to downsample the scene texture before using it for lens flare effect. Lower values will use higher resolution texture for calculating lens flare, at the cost of lower performance. Valid range is [1, 6], default is 4.

property.name: Threshold
property.doc: Determines the minimal threshold of pixel luminance to be included in the lens flare calculations. Any pixel with luminance below this value will be ignored for the purposes of lens flare. Set to zero or negative to disable the threshold and include all pixels in the calculations.

property.name: GhostCount
property.doc: Determines the number of ghost features to appear, shown as blurred blobs of bright areas of the scene.

property.name: GhostSpacing
property.doc: Determines the distance between ghost features. Value is in normalized screen space, in range [0,1] where 1 represents the full screen length.

property.name: Brightness
property.doc: Determines the brightness of the lens flare effect. Value of 1 means the lens flare will be displayed at the same intensity as the scene it was derived from. In range [0, 1], default being 0.05.

property.name: FilterSize
property.doc: Determines the size of the filter when blurring the lens flare features. Larger values yield a blurrier image and will require more performance.

property.name: Halo
property.doc: Determines if a halo effect should be rendered as part of the lens flare.

property.name: HaloRadius
property.doc: Determines how far away from the screen center does the halo appear, in normalized screen space (range [0,1]) where 0.5 represents half screen length.

property.name: HaloThickness
property.doc: Determines the thickness of the halo ring. In normalized screen space (range [0.01,0.5]).

property.name: HaloThreshold
property.doc: Determines the minimal threshold of pixel luminance to be included for halo generation. Any pixel with luminance below this value will be ignored for the purposes of halo generation.

property.name: HaloAspectRatio
property.doc: Determines the shape of the halo. Set to value other than 1 to make the halo an oval rather than a circle.

property.name: ChromaticAberration
property.doc: Enables or disables chromatic aberration of the lens flare and halo features. Chromatic aberration separates the values of red, green and blue channels according to a user provided offset.

property.name: ChromaticAberrationOffset
property.doc: Determines the distance between pixels within which to sample different channels. The value is in UV coordinates, range [0, 1].

property.name: BicubicUpsampling
property.doc: Uses a higher quality upscaling when blending the lens flare features with scene color. Results in less blocky artifacts at a cost to performance.

enum.doc: Determines which collision events will be reported by physics objects.

enumentry.doc: No collision events will be triggered.
Collision events will be triggered when object enters and/or leaves collision.
Collision events will be triggered when object enters and/or leaves collision, but also every frame the object remains in collision.

struct.doc: Hit information from a physics query.

field.name: Point
field.doc: Position of the hit in world space.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Normal to the surface that was hit.

field.name: Uv
field.doc: Barycentric coordinates of the triangle that was hit (only applicable when triangle meshes are hit).

field.name: Distance
field.doc: Distance from the query origin to the hit position.

field.name: TriangleIdx
field.doc: Index of the triangle that was hit (only applicable when triangle meshes are hit).

field.name: UnmappedTriangleIdx
field.doc: Unmapped index of the triangle that was hit (only applicable when triangle meshes are hit). It represents an index into the original MeshData used to create the PhysicsMesh associated with . In contrast, is only a valid index for the MeshData directly obtained from #collider which can differ from the original MeshData due to the internal implementation.

field.name: Collider
field.doc: Component of the collider that was hit. This may be null if the hit collider has no owner component, in which case refer to #colliderRaw.

struct.doc: Descriptor that describes a simple sprite sheet animation. The parent area is split into a grid of x , each representing one frame of the animation. Every frame is of equal size. Frames are sequentially evaluated starting from the top-most row, iterating over all columns in a row and then moving to next row, up to frames. frames are evaluated every second, allowing you to control animation speed.

field.name: RowCount
field.doc: Number of rows to divide the parent area in. Determines height of the individual frame (depends on parent area size).

field.name: ColumnCount
field.doc: Number of columns to divide the parent area in. Determines column of the individual frame (depends on parent area size).

field.name: FrameCount
field.doc: Number of frames in the animation. Must be less or equal than * .

field.name: FramesPerSecond
field.doc: How many frames to evaluate each second. Determines the animation speed.

enum.doc: Flags that describe how is a texture used.

enumentry.doc: A regular texture that is not often or ever updated from the CPU.
Texture that can be rendered to by the GPU.
A regular texture that is often updated by the CPU.
Texture used as a depth/stencil buffer by the GPU.
Allows you to retrieve views of the texture using a format different from one specified on creation.
Allows the CPU to directly read the texture data buffers from the GPU.
All mesh data will also be cached in CPU memory, making it available for fast read access from the CPU.
Texture that allows load/store operations from the GPU program.

class.doc: Abstract class representing a texture. Specific render systems have their own Texture implementations. Internally represented as one or more surfaces with pixels in a certain number of dimensions, backed by a hardware buffer.

method.doc: Reads internal texture data into a newly allocated buffer.

param.name: face
param.doc: Texture face to read from.

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mipmap level to read from.

returns.doc: Async operation object that will contain the buffer with the data once the operation completes.

method.doc: Returns pixels for the specified mip level & face. Pixels will be read from system memory, which means the texture has to be created with TextureUsage.CPUCached. If the texture was updated from the GPU the pixels retrieved from this method will not reflect that, and you should use GetGPUPixels instead.

param.name: face
param.doc: Face to read the pixels from. Cubemap textures have six faces whose face indices are as specified in the CubeFace enum. Array textures can have an arbitrary number of faces (if it's a cubemap array it has to be a multiple of 6).

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mip level to retrieve pixels for. Top level (0) is the highest quality.

returns.doc: A set of pixels for the specified mip level.

method.doc: Sets pixels for the specified mip level and face.

param.name: data
param.doc: Pixels to assign to the specified mip level. Pixel data must match the mip level size and texture pixel format.

param.name: face
param.doc: Face to write the pixels to. Cubemap textures have six faces whose face indices are as specified in the CubeFace enum. Array textures can have an arbitrary number of faces (if it's a cubemap array it has to be a multiple of 6).

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mip level to set pixels for. Top level (0) is the highest quality.

method.doc: Sets pixels for the specified mip level and face.

param.name: colors
param.doc: Pixels to assign to the specified mip level. Size of the array must match the mip level dimensions. Data is expected to be laid out row by row. Pixels will be automatically converted to the valid pixel format.

param.name: face
param.doc: Face to write the pixels to. Cubemap textures have six faces whose face indices are as specified in the CubeFace enum. Array textures can have an arbitrary number of faces (if it's a cubemap array it has to be a multiple of 6).

param.name: mipLevel
param.doc: Mip level to set pixels for. Top level (0) is the highest quality.

class.doc: Settings that control automatic exposure (eye adaptation) post-process.

property.name: HistogramLog2Min
property.doc: Determines minimum luminance value in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Any luminance value below this value will not be included in the histogram and ignored in scene brightness calculations. In log2 units (-8 = 1/256). In the range [-16, 0].

property.name: HistogramLog2Max
property.doc: Determines maximum luminance value in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Any luminance value above this value will not be included in the histogram and ignored in scene brightness calculations. In log2 units (4 = 16). In the range [0, 16].

property.name: HistogramPctLow
property.doc: Percentage below which to ignore values in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Total luminance in the histogram will be summed up and multiplied by this value to calculate minimal luminance. Luminance values below the minimal luminance will be ignored and not used in scene brightness calculations. This allows you to remove outliers on the lower end of the histogram (for example a few very dark pixels in an otherwise bright image). In range [0.0f, 1.0f].

property.name: HistogramPctHigh
property.doc: Percentage above which to ignore values in the eye adaptation histogram. The histogram is used for calculating the average brightness of the scene. Total luminance in the histogram will be summed up and multiplied by this value to calculate maximum luminance. Luminance values above the maximum luminance will be ignored and not used in scene brightness calculations. This allows you to remove outliers on the high end of the histogram (for example a few very bright pixels). In range [0.0f, 1.0f].

property.name: MinEyeAdaptation
property.doc: Clamps the minimum eye adaptation scale to this value. This allows you to limit eye adaptation so that exposure is never too high (for example when in a very dark room you probably do not want the exposure to be so high that everything is still visible). In range [0.0f, 10.0f].

property.name: MaxEyeAdaptation
property.doc: Clamps the maximum eye adaptation scale to this value. This allows you to limit eye adaptation so that exposure is never too low (for example when looking at a very bright light source you probably don't want the exposure to be so low that the rest of the scene is all white (overexposed). In range [0.0f, 10.0f].

property.name: EyeAdaptationSpeedUp
property.doc: Determines how quickly does the eye adaptation adjust to larger values. This affects how quickly does the automatic exposure changes when the scene brightness increases. In range [0.01f, 20.0f].

property.name: EyeAdaptationSpeedDown
property.doc: Determines how quickly does the eye adaptation adjust to smaller values. This affects how quickly does the automatic exposure changes when the scene brightness decreases. In range [0.01f, 20.0f].

class.doc: Settings that control tonemap post-process.

property.name: FilmicCurveShoulderStrength
property.doc: Controls the shoulder (upper non-linear) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects bright areas of the image and allows you to reduce over-exposure.

property.name: FilmicCurveLinearStrength
property.doc: Controls the linear (middle) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects mid-range areas of the image.

property.name: FilmicCurveLinearAngle
property.doc: Controls the linear (middle) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects mid-range areas of the image and allows you to control how quickly does the curve climb.

property.name: FilmicCurveToeStrength
property.doc: Controls the toe (lower non-linear) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Mostly affects dark areas of the image and allows you to reduce under-exposure.

property.name: FilmicCurveToeNumerator
property.doc: Controls the toe (lower non-linear) section of the filmic curve. used for tonemapping. Affects low-range.

property.name: FilmicCurveToeDenominator
property.doc: Controls the toe (lower non-linear) section of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Affects low-range.

property.name: FilmicCurveLinearWhitePoint
property.doc: Controls the white point of the filmic curve used for tonemapping. Affects the entire curve.

class.doc: Settings that control white balance post-process.

property.name: Temperature
property.doc: Temperature used for white balancing, in Kelvins. Moves along the Planckian locus. In range [1500.0f, 15000.0f].

property.name: Tint
property.doc: Additional tint to be applied during white balancing. Can be used to further tweak the white balancing effect by modifying the tint of the light. The tint is chosen on the Planckian locus isothermal, depending on the light temperature specified by #temperature. In range [-1.0f, 1.0f].

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how is a shader imported.

method.doc: Sets a define and its value. Replaces an existing define if one already exists with the provided name.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define.

param.name: value
param.doc: Value to assign to the define.

method.doc: Checks if the define exists and returns its value if it does.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define to get the value for.

param.name: value
param.doc: value of the define. Only defined if the method returns true.

returns.doc: True if the define was found, false otherwise.

method.doc: Checks if the provided define exists.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define to check.

returns.doc: True if the define was found, false otherwise.

method.doc: Unregisters a previously set define.

param.name: define
param.doc: Name of the define to unregister.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are meshes imported.

property.name: Languages
property.doc: Flags that control which shading languages should the BSL shader be converted into. This ultimately controls on which render backends it will be able to run on.

class.doc: Settings that control color grading post-process.

property.name: Saturation
property.doc: Saturation to be applied during color grading. Larger values increase vibrancy of the image. In range [0.0f, 2.0f].

property.name: Contrast
property.doc: Contrast to be applied during color grading. Larger values increase difference between light and dark areas of the image. In range [0.0f, 2.0f].

property.name: Gain
property.doc: Gain to be applied during color grading. Simply increases all color values by an equal scale. In range [0.0f, 2.0f].

property.name: Offset
property.doc: Gain to be applied during color grading. Simply offsets all color values by an equal amount. In range [-1.0f, 1.0f].

class.doc: Settings that control screen space ambient occlusion.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the screen space ambient occlusion effect.

property.name: Radius
property.doc: Radius (in world space, in meters) over which occluders are searched for. Smaller radius ensures better sampling precision but can miss occluders. Larger radius ensures far away occluders are considered but can yield lower quality or noise because of low sampling precision. Usually best to keep at around a meter, valid range is roughly [0.05, 5.0].

property.name: Bias
property.doc: Bias used to reduce false occlusion artifacts. Higher values reduce the amount of artifacts but will cause details to be lost in areas where occlusion isn't high. Value is in millimeters. Usually best to keep at a few dozen millimeters, valid range is roughly [0, 200].

property.name: FadeDistance
property.doc: Distance (in view space, in meters) after which AO starts fading out. The fade process will happen over the range as specified by .

property.name: FadeRange
property.doc: Range (in view space, in meters) in which AO fades out from 100% to 0%. AO starts fading out after the distance specified in .

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Linearly scales the intensity of the AO effect. Values less than 1 make the AO effect less pronounced, and vice versa. Valid range is roughly [0.2, 2].

property.name: Power
property.doc: Controls how quickly does the AO darkening effect increase with higher occlusion percent. This is a non-linear control and will cause the darkening to ramp up exponentially. Valid range is roughly [1, 4], where 1 means no extra darkening will occur.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Quality level of generated ambient occlusion. In range [0, 4]. Higher levels yield higher quality AO at the cost of performance.

enum.doc: Types of available depth of field effects.

enumentry.doc: Fastest depth of field variant, uses gaussian blur to approximate depth of field on near and far objects, but doesn't use any physically based methods for determining blur amount.

Very expensive depth of field variant that allows you to use a bokeh texture, controlling the shape of the blur (usually intended to mimic real world camera aperature shapes). Blur is varied according to actual object distance and the effect is more physically based than gaussian blur (but not completely). Very expensive in terms of performance.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how is a file containing script source code importer.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how is script code imported.

property.name: EditorScript
property.doc: Determines whether the script is editor-only or a normal game script.

class.doc: Settings that control the depth-of-field effect.

property.name: BokehShape
property.doc: Texture to use for the bokeh shape. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the depth of field effect.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Type of depth of field effect to use.

property.name: FocalDistance
property.doc: Distance from the camera at which the focal plane is located in. Objects at this distance will be fully in focus. In world units (meters).

property.name: FocalRange
property.doc: Range within which the objects remain fully in focus. This range is applied relative to the focal distance. This parameter should usually be non-zero when using the Gaussian depth of field effect. When using other types of depth-of-field you can set this to zero for a more physically-based effect, or keep it non-zero for more artistic control. In world units (meters).

property.name: NearTransitionRange
property.doc: Determines the size of the range within which objects transition from focused to fully unfocused, at the near plane. Only relevant for Gaussian and Bokeh depth of field. In world units (meters).

property.name: FarTransitionRange
property.doc: Determines the size of the range within which objects transition from focused to fully unfocused, at the far plane. Only relevant for Gaussian and Bokeh depth of field. In world units (meters).

property.name: NearBlurAmount
property.doc: Determines the amount of blur to apply to fully unfocused objects that are closer to camera than the in-focus zone. Set to zero to disable near-field blur. Only relevant for Gaussian depth of field.

property.name: FarBlurAmount
property.doc: Determines the amount of blur to apply to fully unfocused objects that are farther away from camera than the in-focus zone. Set to zero to disable far-field blur. Only relevant for Gaussian depth of field.

property.name: MaxBokehSize
property.doc: Determines the maximum size of the blur kernel, in percent of view size. Larger values cost more performance. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: AdaptiveColorThreshold
property.doc: Determines the maximum color difference between surrounding pixels allowed (as a sum of all channels) before higher fidelity sampling is triggered. Increasing this value can improve performance as less higher fidelity samples will be required, but may decrease quality of the effect. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: AdaptiveRadiusThreshold
property.doc: Determines the minimum circle of confusion size before higher fidelity sampling is triggered. Small values trigger high fidelity sampling because they can otherwise produce aliasing, and they are small enough so they don't cost much. Increasing this value can improve performance as less higher fidelity samples will be required, but may decrease quality of the effect. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: ApertureSize
property.doc: Camera aperture size in mm. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: FocalLength
property.doc: Focal length of the camera's lens (e.g. 75mm). Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: SensorSize
property.doc: Camera sensor width and height, in mm. Used for controlling the size of the circle of confusion. Only relevant when using Bokeh depth of field.

property.name: BokehOcclusion
property.doc: Set to true if Bokeh flare should use soft depth testing to ensure they don't render on top of foreground geometry. This can help reduce background bleeding into the foreground, which can be especially noticeable if the background is much brighter than the foreground. Use to tweak the effect.

property.name: OcclusionDepthRange
property.doc: Range in world units over which range to fade out Bokeh flare influence. Influence of the flare will be faded out as the depth difference between the flare's origin pixel and the destination pixel grows larger. Only relevant if is turned on.

enum.doc: Determines which parts of the scene will trigger motion blur.

enumentry.doc: Camera movement and rotation will result in full-screen motion blur.

Object movement and rotation will result in blurring of the moving object. Can be significantly more expensive than just using camera blur due to the requirement to use a velocity buffer (unless some other effect also requires it, in which case it will be re-used).
Both the camera movement and object movement will result in motion blur.

class.doc: GUI element representing a toggle (on/off) button.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

struct.doc: Data about a sub-mesh range and the type of primitives contained in the range.

class.doc: A set of animation events that will be added to an animation clip during animation import.

enum.doc: Type of filter to use when filtering samples contributing to a blurred pixel.

enumentry.doc: Samples will be simply averaged together to create the blurred pixel.

A more advanced reconstruction filter will be used. This filter provides better blur quality at a performance cost. In particular the filter will improve blurring at object boundaries, allowing blur to extend beyond the object silhouette. It will also try to estimate blurred background and provide better weighting between background, center and foreground samples.

enum.doc: Determines the number of samples to take during motion blur filtering.

enumentry.doc: 4 samples per pixel.
6 samples per pixel.
8 samples per pixel.
12 samples per pixel.
16 samples per pixel.

class.doc: Settings that control the screen space reflections effect. Screen space reflections provide high quality mirror-like reflections at low performance cost. They should be used together with reflection probes as the effects complement each other. As the name implies, the reflections are only limited to geometry drawn on the screen and the system will fall back to refl. probes when screen space data is unavailable. Similarly the system will fall back to refl. probes for rougher (more glossy rather than mirror-like) surfaces. Those surfaces require a higher number of samples to achieve the glossy look, so we instead fall back to refl. probes which are pre-filtered and can be quickly sampled.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the SSR effect.

property.name: Quality
property.doc: Quality of the SSR effect. Higher values cast more sample rays, and march those rays are lower increments for better precision. This results in higher quality, as well as a higher performance requirement. Valid range is [0, 4], default is 2.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Intensity of the screen space reflections. Valid range is [0, 1]. Default is 1 (100%).

property.name: MaxRoughness
property.doc: Roughness at which screen space reflections start fading out and become replaced with refl. probes. Valid range is [0, 1]. Default is 0.8.

class.doc: Settings that control the chromatic aberration effect.

property.name: FringeTexture
property.doc: Optional texture to apply to generate the channel shift fringe, allowing you to modulate the shifted colors. This texture should be 3x1 size, where the first pixel modules red, second green and third blue channel. If using the complex aberration effect you can use a larger texture, Nx1 size.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the effect.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Type of algorithm to use for rendering the effect.

property.name: ShiftAmount
property.doc: Determines the brightness of the lens flare effect. Value of 1 means the lens flare will be displayed at the same intensity as the scene it was derived from. In range [0, 1], default being 0.05.

class.doc: Settings that control the film grain effect. Film grains adds a time-varying noise effect over the entire image.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Enables or disables the effect.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Controls how intense are the displayed film grains.

property.name: Speed
property.doc: Controls at what speed do the film grains change.

class.doc: Various options that control shadow rendering for a specific view.

property.name: DirectionalShadowDistance
property.doc: Maximum distance that directional light shadows are allowed to render at. Decreasing the distance can yield higher quality shadows nearer to the viewer, as the shadow map resolution isn't being used up on far away portions of the scene. In world units (meters).

property.name: NumCascades
property.doc: Number of cascades to use for directional shadows. Higher number of cascades increases shadow quality as each individual cascade has less area to cover, but can significantly increase performance cost, as well as a minor increase in memory cost. Valid range is roughly [1, 6].

property.name: CascadeDistributionExponent
property.doc: Allows you to control how are directional shadow cascades distributed. Value of 1 means the cascades will be linearly split, each cascade taking up the same amount of space. Value of 2 means each subsequent split will be twice the size of the previous one (meaning cascades closer to the viewer cover a smaller area, and therefore yield higher resolution shadows). Higher values increase the size disparity between near and far cascades at an exponential rate. Valid range is roughly [1, 4].

property.name: ShadowFilteringQuality
property.doc: Determines the number of samples used for percentage closer shadow map filtering. Higher values yield higher quality shadows, at the cost of performance. Valid range is [1, 4].

class.doc: Settings that control rendering for a specific camera (view).

property.name: DepthOfField
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the gaussian depth of field effect.

property.name: ChromaticAberration
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the chromatic aberration effect.

property.name: EnableAutoExposure
property.doc: Determines should automatic exposure be applied to the HDR image. When turned on the average scene brightness will be calculated and used to automatically expose the image to the optimal range. Use the parameters provided by autoExposure to customize the automatic exposure effect. You may also use exposureScale to manually adjust the automatic exposure. When automatic exposure is turned off you can use exposureScale to manually set the exposure.

property.name: AutoExposure
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing automatic scene exposure.

property.name: EnableTonemapping
property.doc: Determines should the image be tonemapped. Tonemapping converts an HDR image into LDR image by applying a filmic curve to the image, simulating the effect of film cameras. Filmic curve improves image quality by tapering off lows and highs, preventing under- and over-exposure. This is useful if an image contains both very dark and very bright areas, in which case the global exposure parameter would leave some areas either over- or under-exposed. Use #tonemapping to customize how tonemapping performed. If this is disabled, then color grading and white balancing will not be enabled either. Only relevant for HDR images.

property.name: Tonemapping
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing tonemapping.

property.name: WhiteBalance
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing white balancing. White balancing converts a scene illuminated by a light of the specified temperature into a scene illuminated by a standard D65 illuminant (average midday light) in order to simulate the effects of chromatic adaptation of the human visual system.

property.name: ColorGrading
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing color grading.

property.name: AmbientOcclusion
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing screen space ambient occlusion.

property.name: ScreenSpaceReflections
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing screen space reflections.

property.name: Bloom
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the bloom effect.

property.name: ScreenSpaceLensFlare
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the screen space lens flare effect.

property.name: FilmGrain
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the film grain effect.

property.name: MotionBlur
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the motion blur effect.

property.name: TemporalAa
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing the temporal anti-aliasing effect.

property.name: EnableFxaa
property.doc: Enables the fast approximate anti-aliasing effect.

property.name: ExposureScale
property.doc: Log2 value to scale the eye adaptation by (for example 2^0 = 1). Smaller values yield darker image, while larger yield brighter image. Allows you to customize exposure manually, applied on top of eye adaptation exposure (if enabled). In range [-8, 8].

property.name: Gamma
property.doc: Gamma value to adjust the image for. Larger values result in a brighter image. When tonemapping is turned on the best gamma curve for the output device is chosen automatically and this value can by used to merely tweak that curve. If tonemapping is turned off this is the exact value of the gamma curve that will be applied.

property.name: EnableHdr
property.doc: High dynamic range allows light intensity to be more correctly recorded when rendering by allowing for a larger range of values. The stored light is then converted into visible color range using exposure and a tone mapping operator.

property.name: EnableLighting
property.doc: Determines if scene objects will be lit by lights. If disabled everything will be rendered using their albedo texture with no lighting applied.

property.name: EnableShadows
property.doc: Determines if shadows cast by lights should be rendered. Only relevant if lighting is turned on.

property.name: EnableVelocityBuffer
property.doc: Determines if the G-Buffer should contain per-pixel velocity information. This can be useful if you are rendering an effect that requires this information. Note that effects such as motion blur or temporal anti-aliasing might force the velocity buffer to be enabled regardless of this setting.

property.name: ShadowSettings
property.doc: Parameters used for customizing shadow rendering.

property.name: EnableIndirectLighting
property.doc: Determines if indirect lighting (e.g. from light probes or the sky) is rendered.

property.name: OverlayOnly
property.doc: Signals the renderer to only render overlays (like GUI), and not scene objects. Such rendering doesn't require depth buffer or multi-sampled render targets and will not render any scene objects. This can improve performance and memory usage for overlay-only views.

property.name: EnableSkybox
property.doc: If enabled the camera will use the skybox for rendering the background. A skybox has to be present in the scene. When disabled the camera will use the clear color for rendering the background.

property.name: CullDistance
property.doc: The absolute base cull-distance for objects rendered through this camera in world units. Objects will use this distance and apply their own factor to it to determine whether they should be visible.

enum.doc: Flags for controlling Camera options.

enumentry.doc: If set the camera will only render when requested by the user through Camera::notifyNeedsRedraw(). Otherwise the camera will render every frame (unless disabled).

property.name: Normal
property.doc: Normal vector that determines how is the capsule oriented.

property.name: Center
property.doc: Determines the position of the capsule shape, relative to the component's scene object.

enum.doc: Controls climbing behaviour for a capsule character controller. Normally the character controller will not automatically climb when heights are greater than the assigned step offset. However due to the shape of the capsule it might automatically climb over slightly larger heights than assigned step offsets.

enumentry.doc: Normal behaviour. Capsule character controller will be able to auto-step even above the step offset.
The system will attempt to limit auto-step to the provided step offset and no higher.

class.doc: Image that references a part of a texture by specifying an UV range. When the sprite image is rendered only the portion of the texture specified by the UV range will be rendered. Sprite images also allow you to specify sprite sheet animation by varying which portion of the UV is selected over time.

property.name: Size
property.doc: Returns the pixel size of the UV subrange covered in the texture atlas. If the image includes animation, this will return the size of the entire animation grid.

property.name: AnimationFrameSize
property.doc: Returns the size of a single animation frame in pixels. If the texture has no animation this is the same as GetWidth().

property.name: Texture
property.doc: Retrieves the atlas texture where the image is stored.

property.name: UVRange
property.doc: Determines the UV range that the image is referencing.

property.name: Animation
property.doc: Sets properties describing sprite animation. The animation splits the sprite area into a grid of sub-images which can be evaluated over time. In order to view the animation you must also enable playback through setAnimationPlayback().

property.name: AnimationPlayback
property.doc: Determines if and how should the sprite animation play.

enum.doc: Controls behaviour when a character controller reaches a slope thats larger than its slope offset.

enumentry.doc: Character will be prevented from going further, but will be allowed to move laterally.
Character will be prevented from going further, but also slide down the slope.

class.doc: Applies an arbitrary force to the particles.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle force evolver.
Creates a new particle force evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

enum.doc: Reports in which directions is the character colliding with other objects.

enumentry.doc: Character is colliding with its sides.
Character is colliding with the ceiling.
Character is colliding with the ground.

struct.doc: Contains separate GUI content images for every possible GUI element state.
Contains data about a collision of a character controller and another object.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact position.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Contact normal.

field.name: MotionDir
field.doc: Direction of motion after the hit.

field.name: MotionAmount
field.doc: Magnitude of motion after the hit.

struct.doc: Contains data about a collision of a character controller and a collider.

field.name: Collider
field.doc: Component of the controller that was touched. Can be null if the controller has no component parent, in which case check #colliderRaw.

field.name: TriangleIndex
field.doc: Touched triangle index for mesh colliders.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact position.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Contact normal.

field.name: MotionDir
field.doc: Direction of motion after the hit.

field.name: MotionAmount
field.doc: Magnitude of motion after the hit.

class.doc: Contains mesh vertex and index data used for initializing, updating and reading mesh data from Mesh.

property.name: Positions
property.doc: An array of all vertex positions. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex positions.

property.name: Normals
property.doc: An array of all vertex normals. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex normals.

property.name: Tangents
property.doc: An array of all vertex tangents. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex tangents.

property.name: Colors
property.doc: An array of all vertex colors. Only valid if the vertex layout contains vertex colors.

property.name: UV0
property.doc: An array of all vertex texture coordinates in the UV0 channel. Only valid if the vertex layout contains UV0 coordinates.

property.name: UV1
property.doc: An array of all vertex texture coordinates in the UV1 channel. Only valid if the vertex layout contains UV1 coordinates.

property.name: BoneWeights
property.doc: An array of all vertex bone weights. Only valid if the vertex layout contains bone weights.

property.name: Indices
property.doc: An array of all indices.

property.name: VertexCount
property.doc: Returns the number of vertices contained in the mesh.

property.name: IndexCount
property.doc: Returns the number of indices contained in the mesh.

enum.doc: Type of playback to use for an animation of a SpriteTexture.

enumentry.doc: Do not animate.
Animate once until the end of the animation is reached.
Animate to the end of the animation then loop around.
Loop the animation but reverse playback when the end is reached.

struct.doc: Contains data about a collision between two character controllers.

field.name: Controller
field.doc: Component of the controller that was touched. Can be null if the controller has no component parent, in which case check #controllerRaw.

field.name: Position
field.doc: Contact position.

field.name: Normal
field.doc: Contact normal.

field.name: MotionDir
field.doc: Direction of motion after the hit.

field.name: MotionAmount
field.doc: Magnitude of motion after the hit.

struct.doc: Information about a single probe in the light probe volume.

enum.doc: Specifies first or second body referenced by a Joint.

enumentry.doc: Body the joint is influencing.
Body the joint is attached to (if any).

struct.doc: Controls spring parameters for a physics joint limits. If a limit is soft (body bounces back due to restition when the limit is reached) the spring will pull the body back towards the limit using the specified parameters.

ctor.doc: Constructs a spring with no force.
Constructs a spring.

param.name: stiffness
param.doc: Spring strength. Force proportional to the position error.

param.name: damping
param.doc: Damping strength. Force propertional to the velocity error.

field.name: Stiffness
field.doc: Spring strength. Force proportional to the position error.

field.name: Damping
field.doc: Damping strength. Force propertional to the velocity error.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleForce object.

field.name: Force
field.doc: Determines the force of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: WorldSpace
field.doc: True if the force is provided in world space, false if in local space.

struct.doc: Contains common values used by all Joint limit types.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit between two distance values. Lower value must be less than the upper value.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty limit.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower distance of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper distance of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: lower
param.doc: Lower distance of the limit. Must be less than .

param.name: upper
param.doc: Upper distance of the limit. Must be more than .

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Lower
field.doc: Lower distance of the limit. Must be less than #upper.

field.name: Upper
field.doc: Upper distance of the limit. Must be more than #lower.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit between zero a single distance value.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty limit.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: extent
param.doc: Distance at which the limit becomes active.

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: extent
param.doc: Distance at which the limit becomes active.

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Extent
field.doc: Distance at which the limit becomes active.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

struct.doc: Represents a joint limit that contraints movement to within an elliptical cone.

ctor.doc: Constructs a limit with a 45 degree cone.
Constructs a hard limit. Once the limit is reached the movement of the attached bodies will come to a stop.

param.name: yLimitAngle
param.doc: Y angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Y axis.

param.name: zLimitAngle
param.doc: Z angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Z axis.

param.name: contactDist
param.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit. Specify -1 for the default.

ctor.doc: Constructs a soft limit. Once the limit is reached the bodies will bounce back according to the resitution parameter and will be pulled back towards the limit by the provided spring.

param.name: yLimitAngle
param.doc: Y angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Y axis.

param.name: zLimitAngle
param.doc: Z angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Z axis.

param.name: spring
param.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: YLimitAngle
field.doc: Y angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Y axis.

field.name: ZLimitAngle
field.doc: Z angle of the cone. Movement is constrainted between 0 and this angle on the Z axis.

field.name: ContactDist
field.doc: Distance from the limit at which it becomes active. Allows the solver to activate earlier than the limit is reached to avoid breaking the limit.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Controls how do objects react when the limit is reached, values closer to zero specify non-ellastic collision, while those closer to one specify more ellastic (i.e bouncy) collision. Must be in [0, 1] range.

field.name: Spring
field.doc: Spring that controls how are the bodies pulled back towards the limit when they breach it.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a circle. Using the thickness parameter you can control whether to emit only from circle edge, the entire surface or just a part of the surface. Using the arc parameter you can emit from a specific angular portion of the circle.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter circle shape.
Creates a new particle emitter circle shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

enum.doc: Specifies axes that the D6 joint can constrain motion on.

enumentry.doc: Movement on the X axis.
Movement on the Y axis.
Movement on the Z axis.
Rotation around the X axis.
Rotation around the Y axis.
Rotation around the Z axis.

enum.doc: Specifies type of constraint placed on a specific axis.

enumentry.doc: Axis is immovable.
Axis will be constrained by the specified limits.
Axis will not be constrained.

enum.doc: Type of drives that can be used for moving or rotating bodies attached to the joint.

enumentry.doc: Linear movement on the X axis using the linear drive model.
Linear movement on the Y axis using the linear drive model.
Linear movement on the Z axis using the linear drive model.
Rotation around the Y axis using the twist/swing angular drive model. Should not be used together with SLERP mode.
Rotation around the Z axis using the twist/swing angular drive model. Should not be used together with SLERP mode.

Rotation using spherical linear interpolation. Uses the SLERP angular drive mode which performs rotation by interpolating the quaternion values directly over the shortest path (applies to all three axes, which they all must be unlocked).

Rotation using spherical linear interpolation. Uses the SLERP angular drive mode which performs rotation by interpolating the quaternion values directly over the shortest path (applies to all three axes, which they all must be unlocked).

struct.doc: Specifies parameters for a drive that will attempt to move the joint bodies to the specified drive position and velocity.

field.name: Stiffness
field.doc: Spring strength. Force proportional to the position error.

field.name: Damping
field.doc: Damping strength. Force propertional to the velocity error.

field.name: ForceLimit
field.doc: Maximum force the drive can apply.

field.name: Acceleration
field.doc: If true the drive will generate acceleration instead of forces. Acceleration drives are easier to tune as they account for the masses of the actors to which the joint is attached.

enum.doc: Flags that control options for the spherical joint

enumentry.doc: Enables the cone range limit.

class.doc: Applies linear velocity to the particles.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle velocity evolver.
Creates a new particle velocity evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

class.doc: Wraps Decal as a Component.

enum.doc: Determines how is text horizontally aligned in a GUI element.
Controls distance joint options.

enumentry.doc: Enables minimum distance limit.
Enables maximum distance limit.
Enables spring when maintaining limits.

enum.doc: Flags that control options of a Rigidbody object.

enumentry.doc: No options.
Automatically calculate center of mass transform and inertia tensors from child shapes (colliders).
Calculate mass distribution from child shapes (colliders). Only relevant when auto-tensors is on.
Enables continous collision detection. This can prevent fast moving bodies from tunneling through each other. This must also be enabled globally in Physics otherwise the flag will be ignored.

enum.doc: Flags that control hinge joint options.

enumentry.doc: Joint limit is enabled.
Joint drive is enabled.

struct.doc: Properties of a drive that drives the joint's angular velocity towards a paricular value.

field.name: Speed
field.doc: Target speed of the joint.

field.name: ForceLimit
field.doc: Maximum torque the drive is allowed to apply .

field.name: GearRatio
field.doc: Scales the velocity of the first body, and its response to drive torque is scaled down.

field.name: FreeSpin
field.doc: If the joint is moving faster than the drive's target speed, the drive will try to break. If you don't want the breaking to happen set this to true.

class.doc: Specifies a color as a distribution, which can include a constant color, random color range or a color gradient.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty distribution.
Creates a new distribution that returns a constant color.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a color gradient.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in a range determined by two gradients.

method.doc: Returns the constant value of the distribution, or the minimal value of a constant range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a gradient.
Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-gradient range.
Returns the gradient representing the distribution, or the first gradient representing a gradient range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-gradient range.
Returns the curve representing the second gradient of a gradient range. Only defined if the distribution represents a gradient range.

property.name: DistributionType
property.doc: Returns the type of the represented distribution.

class.doc: Specifies a color as a distribution, which can include a constant color, random color range or a color gradient.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty distribution.
Creates a new distribution that returns a constant color.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a color gradient.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random color in a range determined by two gradients.

method.doc: Returns the constant value of the distribution, or the minimal value of a constant range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a gradient.
Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-gradient range.
Returns the gradient representing the distribution, or the first gradient representing a gradient range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-gradient range.
Returns the curve representing the second gradient of a gradient range. Only defined if the distribution represents a gradient range.

property.name: DistributionType
property.doc: Returns the type of the represented distribution.

class.doc: Specifies a value as a distribution, which can include a constant value, random range or a curve.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty distribution.
Creates a new distribution that returns a constant value.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in the specified range.
Creates a new distribution that evaluates a curve.
Creates a new distribution that returns a random value in a range determined by two curves.

method.doc: Returns the constant value of the distribution, or the minimal value of a constant range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a curve.
Returns the maximum value of a constant range. Only defined if the distribution represents a non-curve range.
Returns the curve representing the distribution, or the first curve representing a curve range. Undefined if the distribution is represented by a constant or a non-curve range.
Returns the curve representing the second curve of a curve range. Only defined if the distribution represents a curve range.
Evaluates the value of the distribution.

param.name: t
param.doc: Time at which to evaluate the distribution. This is only relevant if the distribution contains curves.

param.name: factor
param.doc: Value in range [0, 1] that determines how to interpolate between min/max value, if the distribution represents a range. Value of 0 will return the minimum value, while value of 1 will return the maximum value, and interpolate the values in-between.

returns.doc: Evaluated value.

method.doc: Evaluates the value of the distribution.

param.name: t
param.doc: Time at which to evaluate the distribution. This is only relevant if the distribution contains curves.

param.name: factor
param.doc: Random number generator that determines the factor. Factor determines how to interpolate between min/max value, if the distribution represents a range.

returns.doc: Evaluated value.

property.name: DistributionType
property.doc: Returns the type of the represented distribution.
