struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleTextureAnimation object.

field.name: RandomizeRow
field.doc: Randomly pick a row to use for animation when the particle is first spawned. This implies that only a single row of the grid will be used for individual particle's animation.

field.name: NumCycles
field.doc: Number of cycles to loop the animation during particle's lifetime.

class.doc: Provides functionality for particle texture animation. Uses the sprite texture assigned to the particle's material to determine animation properties.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle texture animation evolver.
Creates a new particle texture animation evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

class.doc: Contains information about an instantiated scene.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Name of the scene.

property.name: Root
property.doc: Root object of the scene.

property.name: IsActive
property.doc: Checks is the scene currently active. IF inactive the scene properties aside from the name are undefined.

property.name: Physics
property.doc: Physical representation of the scene, as assigned by the physics sub-system. Exact implementation depends on the physics plugin used.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleOrbit object.

field.name: Center
field.doc: Position of the center around which to orbit. Evaluated over particle system lifetime.

field.name: Velocity
field.doc: Determines the speed of rotation around each axis. The speed is specified in "turns" where 0 = no rotation, 0.5 = 180 degree rotation and 1 = 360 degree rotation. Evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: Radial
field.doc: Speed at which to push or pull the particles towards/away from the center. Evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: WorldSpace
field.doc: True if the properties provided are in world space, false if in local space.

class.doc: Moves particles so that their sprites orbit their center according to the provided offset and rotation values.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle orbit evolver.
Creates a new particle orbit evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleVelocity object.

field.name: Velocity
field.doc: Determines the velocity of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime.

field.name: WorldSpace
field.doc: True if the velocity is provided in world space, false if in local space.

enum.doc: Types of collision modes that ParticleCollisions evolver can operate in.

enumentry.doc: Particles will collide with a user-provided set of planes.
Particles will collide with physics colliders in the scene.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleGravity object.

field.name: Scale
field.doc: Scale which to apply to the gravity value retrieved from the physics sub-system.

enum.doc: Flags used to further describe a shader parameter.

enumentry.doc: Parameter is for internal use by the renderer and isn't expected to be set by the user.
Parameter should not be displayed in the editor inspector.
Allows the color parameter to be edited using the HDR color picker.

class.doc: Provides easy access to variety of icons for use in the engine.

method.doc: Retrieves a particular stock icon.

param.name: icon
param.doc: Icon to retrieve.

param.name: size
param.doc: Size of the icon in points.

method.doc: Returns the unicode character corresponding to an icon.
Returns the font in which the provided icon is stored in.
Parses an icon name and returns the corresponding enum entry if found.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleColor object.

field.name: Color
field.doc: Determines the color of the particles evaluated over particle lifetime.

class.doc: Changes the color of the particles over the particle lifetime.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle color evolver.
Creates a new particle color evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

class.doc: Changes the size of the particles over the particle lifetime.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle size evolver.
Creates a new particle size evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleRotation object.

field.name: Rotation
field.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles in degrees, applied around the particle's local Z axis. Only used if 3D rotation is disabled.

field.name: Rotation3D
field.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles in degrees as Euler angles. Only used if 3D rotation is enabled.

field.name: Use3DRotation
field.doc: Determines should the particle rotation be a single angle applied around a Z axis (if disabled), or a set of Euler angles that allow you to rotate around every axis (if enabled).

class.doc: Rotates the particles over the particle lifetime.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle rotation evolver.
Creates a new particle rotation evolver.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

struct.doc: Structure used for initializing a ParticleCollisions object.

field.name: Mode
field.doc: Collision mode determining with which geometry the particles will interact with.

field.name: Restitution
field.doc: Determines the elasticity (bounciness) of the particle collision. Lower values make the collision less bouncy and higher values more.

field.name: Dampening
field.doc: Determines how much velocity should a particle lose after a collision, in percent of its current velocity. In range [0, 1].

field.name: LifetimeLoss
field.doc: Determines how much should the particle lifetime be reduced after a collision, in percent of its original lifetime. In range [0, 1].

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of every individual particle used for collisions, in meters.

field.name: Layer
field.doc: Physics layers that determine which objects will particle collide with. Only relevant when using the World collision mode.

class.doc: Particle evolver that allows particles to collide with the world.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle collision evolver.
Creates a new particle collision evolver.

property.name: Planes
property.doc: Determines a set of planes to use when using the Plane collision mode. Planes are expected to be in world space.

property.name: PlaneObjects
property.doc: Determines a set of objects whose transforms to derive the collision planes from. Objects can move in the world and collision planes will be updated automatically. Object's negative Z axis is considered to be plane normal.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the evolver.

class.doc: Represents a physics mesh that can be used with a MeshCollider. Physics mesh can be a generic triangle mesh or a convex mesh. Convex meshes are limited to 255 faces.

property.name: Type
property.doc: Returns the type of the physics mesh.

enum.doc: Types of emission modes.

enumentry.doc: Position will be picked randomly on a shape.
Positions will loop around the shape in a predictable fashion.
Similar to Loop, except the order will be reversed when one loop iteration finishes.
All particles spawned on the shape at some instant (usually a frame) will be spread around the shape equally.

struct.doc: Controls how are particle positions on a shape chosen.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Type that determines general behaviour.

field.name: Speed
field.doc: Speed along which particle generation should move around the shape, relevant for Loop and PingPing emission modes.

field.name: Interval
field.doc: Determines the minimum interval allowed between the generated particles. 0 specifies the particles can be generated anywhere on the shape.

class.doc: Base class from all emitter shapes. Emitter shapes determine the position and direction of newly created particles.

enum.doc: Determines the emission type for the cone particle emitter shape.

enumentry.doc: Emit particles only from the cone base.
Emit particles from the entire cone volume.

enum.doc: Determines how is a font rendered into the bitmap texture.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteTexture.

field.name: AtlasTexture
field.doc: Texture used as the atlas.

field.name: UVRange
field.doc: Range in the atlas texture that the image maps to.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterConeShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines where on the cone are the particles emitter from.

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of the cone base.

field.name: Angle
field.doc: Angle of the cone.

field.name: Length
field.doc: Length of the cone. Irrelevant if emission type is Base.

field.name: Thickness
field.doc: Proportion of the volume that can emit particles. Thickness of 0 results in particles being emitted only from the edge of the cone, while thickness of 1 results in particles being emitted from the entire volume. In-between values will use a part of the volume.

field.name: Arc
field.doc: Angular portion of the cone from which to emit particles from, in degrees.

field.name: Mode
field.doc: Determines how will particle positions on the shape be generated.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a cone. Particles can be created on cone base or volume, while controling the radial arc of the emitted portion of the volume, as well as thickness of the cone emission volume. All particles will have random normals within the distribution of the cone.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter cone shape.
Creates a new particle emitter cone shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

enum.doc: Available vertex layouts that specify what data is provided per-vertex in a mesh. Combinations other than those provided are allowed.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterSphereShape.

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of the sphere.

field.name: Thickness
field.doc: Proportion of the volume that can emit particles. Thickness of 0 results in particles being emitted only from the edge of the volume, while thickness of 1 results in particles being emitted from the entire volume. In-between values will use a part of the volume.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a sphere. Particles can be emitted from sphere surface, the entire volume or a proportion of the volume depending on the thickness parameter. All particles will have normals pointing outwards in a spherical direction.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.
Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterHemisphereShape.

field.name: Radius
field.doc: Radius of the hemisphere.

field.name: Thickness
field.doc: Proportion of the volume that can emit particles. Thickness of 0 results in particles being emitted only from the edge of the volume, while thickness of 1 results in particles being emitted from the entire volume. In-between values will use a part of the volume.

class.doc: Base class for a clickable GUI button element.

method.doc: Change content displayed by the button.

event.doc: Triggered when pointer hovers over the button.
Triggered when pointer that was previously hovering leaves the button.
Triggered when button is clicked twice in rapid succession.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a hemisphere. Particles can be emitted from the hemisphere surface, the entire volume or a proportion of the volume depending on the thickness parameter. All particles will have normals pointing outwards in a spherical direction.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.
Creates a new particle emitter sphere shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

class.doc: Contains definitions of GPU programs used for rendering, as well as a set of global parameters to control those programs.

property.name: VariationParams
property.doc: Returns the list of all variation parameters supported by this shader, possible values of each parameter and other meta-data.

property.name: Parameters
property.doc: Returns information about all parameters available in the shader.

enum.doc: Determines the emission type for the cone particle emitter shape.

enumentry.doc: Particles will be emitted from the entire volume.
Particles will be emitted only from box surface.
Particles will be emitted only from box edge.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterBoxShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines from which portion of the box should particles be emitted from.

field.name: Extents
field.doc: Extends of the box.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from an axis aligned box. Particles can be emitted from box volume, surface or edges. All particles have their normals set to positive Z direction.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter box shape.
Creates a new particle emitter box shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterLineShape.

field.name: Length
field.doc: Length of the line.

field.name: Mode
field.doc: Determines how will particle positions on the shape be generated.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from a line segment.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter edge shape.
Creates a new particle emitter edge shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterRectShape.

field.name: Extents
field.doc: Extents of the rectangle.

class.doc: Particle emitter shape that emits particles from the surface of a rectangle.

ctor.doc: Creates a new particle emitter rectangle shape.
Creates a new particle emitter rectangle shape.

property.name: Options
property.doc: Options describing the shape.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a Font.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional name of the font. Used primarily for easier debugging.

field.name: DPI
field.doc: Determines dots per inch scale that will be used when rendering the characters.

field.name: RenderMode
field.doc: Determines the render mode used for rendering the characters into a bitmap.

enum.doc: Determines the emission type for the mesh particle emitter shape.

enumentry.doc: Particles will be emitted from mesh vertices.
Particles will be emitted from mesh edges.
Particles will be emitted from mesh triangles.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterStaticMeshShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines from which portion of the mesh are the particles emitted from.

field.name: Sequential
field.doc: When enabled the particles will be emitted sequentially from mesh vertices in the order they are defined. Only relevant for the Vertex emit mode.

field.name: Mesh
field.doc: Mesh to spawn particles on. Must at least contain per-vertex position data encoded as 3D float vectors. Can optionally contain per-vertex normals encoded as 3D float vectors or as 4-byte unsigned-normalized format.

struct.doc: Information describing a ParticleEmitterSkinnedMeshShape.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Determines from which portion of the mesh are the particles emitted from.

field.name: Sequential
field.doc: When enabled the particles will be emitted sequentially from mesh vertices in the order they are defined. Only relevant for the Vertex emit mode.

field.name: Renderable
field.doc: Renderable object containing a mesh to spawn particles on, as well as the attached Animation object resposible for performing skinned animation. Mesh must at least contain per-vertex position data encoded as 3D float vectors, blend indices encoded in 4-byte format, and blend weights encoded a 4D float vectors. Can optionally contain per-vertex normals encoded as 3D float vectors or as 4-byte unsigned-normalized format.

struct.doc: Specifies a burst of particles that occurs at a certain time point.

field.name: Time
field.doc: Time at which to trigger the burst, in seconds.

field.name: Count
field.doc: Number of particles to emit when the burst triggers.

field.name: Cycles
field.doc: Determines how many times to trigger the burst. If 0 the burst will trigger infinitely. Use to to control the time between each cycle.

field.name: Interval
field.doc: Controls how much time needs to pass before triggering another burst cycle, in seconds.

class.doc: Handles spawning of new particles using the specified parameters and shape.

ctor.doc: Creates a new emitter.

property.name: Shape
property.doc: Shape over which to emit the particles.

property.name: EmissionRate
property.doc: Determines the number of particles that are emitted every second.

property.name: EmissionBursts
property.doc: Determines discrete intervals to emit particles.

property.name: InitialLifetime
property.doc: Determines the lifetime of particles when they are initially spawned, in seconds.

property.name: InitialSpeed
property.doc: Sets the initial speed of the particles, in meters/second. The speed is applied along the particle's velocity direction, which is determined by the emission shape and potentially other properties.

property.name: InitialSize
property.doc: Determines the size of the particles when initially spawned. The size is applied uniformly in all dimensions. Only used if 3D size is disabled.

property.name: InitialSize3D
property.doc: Determines the size of the particles when initially spawned. Size can be specified for each dimension separately. Only used if 3D size is enabled.

property.name: Use3DSize
property.doc: Determines should the initial particle size be applied uniformly (if disabled), or evaluated separately for each dimension (if enabled).

property.name: InitialRotation
property.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles when initially spawned, in degrees. The rotation is applied around the particle's local Z axis. Only used if 3D rotation is disabled.

property.name: InitialRotation3D
property.doc: Determines the rotation of the particles when initially spawned, in Euler angles. Only used if 3D rotation is enabled.

property.name: Use3DRotation
property.doc: Determines should the initial particle rotation be a single angle applied around a Z axis (if disabled), or a set of Euler angles that allow you to rotate around every axis (if enabled).

property.name: InitialColor
property.doc: Determines the initial color (in RGB channels) and transparency (in A channel) of particles.

property.name: RandomOffset
property.doc: Determines a range of values determining a random offset to apply to particle position after it has been emitted. Offset will be randomly selected in all three axes in range [-value, value].

property.name: FlipU
property.doc: Determines should particle U texture coordinate be randomly flipped, mirroring the image. The value represents a percent of particles that should be flipped, in range [0, 1].

property.name: FlipV
property.doc: Determines should particle V texture coordinate be randomly flipped, mirroring the image. The value represents a percent of particles that should be flipped, in range [0, 1].

enum.doc: Possible orientations when rendering billboard particles.

enumentry.doc: Orient towards view (camera) plane.
Orient towards view (camera) position.
Orient with a user-provided axis.

enum.doc: Space in which to spawn/transform particles.

enumentry.doc: Particles will always remain local to their transform parent. This means if the transform parent moves so will all the particles.

Particles will be placed in world space. This means they will spawn at the location of the transform parent, but are no longer affected by its transform after spawn (e.g. smoke rising from a moving train).

enum.doc: Determines how to sort particles before rendering.

enumentry.doc: Do not sort the particles.
Sort by distance from the camera, furthest to nearest.
Sort by age, oldest to youngest.
Sort by age, youngest to oldest.

class.doc: Controls depth buffer collisions for GPU simulated particles.

property.name: Enabled
property.doc: Determines if depth collisions are enabled.

property.name: Restitution
property.doc: Determines the elasticity (bounciness) of the particle collision. Lower values make the collision less bouncy and higher values more.

property.name: Dampening
property.doc: Determines how much velocity should a particle lose after a collision, in percent of its current velocity. In range [0, 1].

property.name: RadiusScale
property.doc: Scale which to apply to particle size in order to determine the collision radius.

class.doc: Settings used for controlling a vector field in a GPU simulated particle system.

property.name: VectorField
property.doc: Vector field resource used for influencing the particles.

property.name: Intensity
property.doc: Intensity of the forces and velocities applied by the vector field.

property.name: Tightness
property.doc: Determines how closely does the particle velocity follow the vectors in the field. If set to 1 particles will be snapped to the exact velocity of the value in the field, and if set to 0 the field will not influence particle velocities directly.

property.name: Scale
property.doc: Scale to apply to the vector field bounds. This is multiplied with the bounds of the vector field resource.

property.name: Offset
property.doc: Amount of to move the vector field by relative to the parent particle system. This is added to the bounds provided in the vector field resource.

property.name: Rotation
property.doc: Initial rotation of the vector field.

property.name: RotationRate
property.doc: Determines the amount to rotate the vector field every second, in degrees, around XYZ axis respectively. Evaluated over the particle system lifetime.

property.name: TilingX
property.doc: Determines should the field influence particles outside of the field bounds. If true the field will be tiled infinitely in the X direction.

property.name: TilingY
property.doc: Determines should the field influence particles outside of the field bounds. If true the field will be tiled infinitely in the Y direction.

property.name: TilingZ
property.doc: Determines should the field influence particles outside of the field bounds. If true the field will be tiled infinitely in the Z direction.

class.doc: Generic settings used for controlling a ParticleSystem.

property.name: Material
property.doc: Material to render the particles with.

property.name: Mesh
property.doc: Mesh used for representing individual particles when using the Mesh rendering mode.

property.name: SimulationSpace
property.doc: Determines in which space are particles in.

property.name: Orientation
property.doc: Determines how are particles oriented when rendering.

property.name: Duration
property.doc: Determines the time period during which the system runs, in seconds. This effects evaluation of distributions with curves using particle system time for evaluation.

property.name: IsLooping
property.doc: Determines should the particle system time wrap around once it reaches its duration.

property.name: MaxParticles
property.doc: Determines the maximum number of particles that can ever be active in this system. This number is ignored if GPU simulation is enabled, and instead particle count is instead only limited by the size of the internal buffers (shared between all particle systems).

property.name: GpuSimulation
property.doc: If true the particle system will be simulated on the GPU. This allows much higher particle counts at lower performance cost. GPU simulation ignores any provided evolvers and instead uses ParticleGpuSimulationSettings to customize the GPU simulation.

property.name: RenderMode
property.doc: Determines how is each particle represented on the screen.

property.name: OrientationLockY
property.doc: Determines should the particles only be allowed to orient themselves around the Y axis, or freely. Ignored if using the Plane orientation mode.

property.name: OrientationPlaneNormal
property.doc: Determines a normal of the plane to orient particles towards. Only used if particle orientation mode is set to ParticleOrientation::Plane.

property.name: SortMode
property.doc: Determines how (and if) are particles sorted. Sorting controls in what order are particles rendered. If GPU simulation is enabled only distance based sorting is supported.

property.name: UseAutomaticSeed
property.doc: Determines should an automatic seed be used for the internal random number generator. This ensures the particle system yields different results each time it is ran.

property.name: ManualSeed
property.doc: Determines the seed to use for the internal random number generator. Allows you to guarantee identical behaviour between different runs. Only relevant if automatic seed is disabled.

property.name: UseAutomaticBounds
property.doc: Determines should the particle system bounds be automatically calculated, or should the fixed value provided be used. Bounds are used primarily for culling purposes. Note that automatic bounds are not supported when GPU simulation is enabled.

property.name: CustomBounds
property.doc: Custom bounds to use them is disabled. The bounds are in the simulation space of the particle system.

class.doc: Settings used for controlling particle system GPU simulation.

property.name: ColorOverLifetime
property.doc: Determines particle color, evaluated over the particle lifetime.

property.name: SizeScaleOverLifetime
property.doc: Determines particle size, evaluated over the particle lifetime. Multiplied by the initial particle size.

property.name: Acceleration
property.doc: Constant acceleration to apply for each step of the simulation.

property.name: Drag
property.doc: Amount of resistance to apply in the direction opposite of the particle's velocity.

property.name: DepthCollision
property.doc: Settings controlling particle depth buffer collisions.

method.doc: Enables or disabled preview mode. Preview mode allows the particle system to play while the game is not running, primarily for preview purposes in the editor. Returns true if the preview mode was enabled, false if it was disabled or enabling preview failed.

property.name: Normal
property.doc: Normal vector that determines the local orientation of the plane.

property.name: Distance
property.doc: Determines the distance of the plane from the local origin, along its normal vector.

enum.doc: Probe type that determines the shape of the probe and how is it interpreted by the renderer.

enumentry.doc: Reflection probe cubemap is generated, and box extents are used for calculating influence ranges and box geometry.
Reflection probe cubemap is generated, but sphere is used for calculating the influence radius and proxy geometry.

property.name: Bounds
property.doc: Gets world bounds of the mesh rendered by this object.

enum.doc: Type of force or torque that can be applied to a rigidbody.

enumentry.doc: Value applied is a force.
Value applied is an impulse (a direct change in its linear or angular momentum).
Value applied is velocity.
Value applied is accelearation.

property.name: CenterOfMassPosition
property.doc: Determines the rigidbody's center of mass position. Only relevant if RigibodyFlag::AutoTensors is turned off.

property.name: CenterOfMassRotation
property.doc: Determines the rigidbody's center of mass rotation. Only relevant if RigibodyFlag::AutoTensors is turned off.

property.name: CollisionReportMode
property.doc: Sets a value that determines which (if any) collision events are reported.

enum.doc: Flag that controls slider joint's behaviour.

enumentry.doc: Enables the linear range limit.

property.name: Center
property.doc: Determines position of the sphere shape, relative to the component's scene object.

struct.doc: Information about a SpriteImage.

field.name: UVRange
field.doc: Range in the atlas texture that the image maps to.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteImage.

field.name: UVRange
field.doc: Range in the atlas texture that the image maps to.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteGlyph.

field.name: Font
field.doc: Font from which to render the glyph from.

field.name: Glyph
field.doc: Unicode code for the glyph to render.

field.name: Size
field.doc: Size of the glyph in points.

field.name: UVRange
field.doc: Range in the atlas texture that the image maps to.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

class.doc: Implementation of SpriteImage that renders a single glyph from a Font.

method.doc: Sets the font to render the glyph from.
Sets the unicode code of the glyph to render.
Sets the size of the glyph in points.

ctor.doc: Creates a new sprite glyph.
Creates a new sprite glyph.

class.doc: Implementation of SpriteImage that uses a user-provided texture as the atlas texture.

method.doc: Sets the atlas texture to utilize.

ctor.doc: Creates a new sprite texture that references the entire area of the provided texture.
Creates a new sprite texture that references a sub-area of the provided texture.

enum.doc: Determines how to scale path canvas when rasterizing for a particular size.

enumentry.doc: Canvas will stretch non-uniformly in both dimensions in order to cover the raster area fully.
Canvas will scale uniformly until one dimension is aligned with the raster area. Remaining dimension might have empty space, and canvas will be placed in the center of the raster dimension.
Canvas will scale uniformly until both dimensions are larger or aligned with the raster area. Remaining dimension might be cropped.
Do not perform any scaling. Canvas size is ignored and path coordinates are mapped 1:1 to raster coordinates.

class.doc: Represents a vector path containing curves and geometric shapes that can be rasterized to any dimension.

struct.doc: Descriptor structure used for initialization of a SpriteVectorPath.

field.name: VectorPath
field.doc: Vector path to render on the sprite.

field.name: Size
field.doc: Size of the rasterized path, in pixels.

field.name: ScalingMode
field.doc: How to scale the path canvas onto the rasterized destination.

field.name: UVRange
field.doc: Range in the atlas texture that the image maps to.

field.name: AnimationPlayback
field.doc: Determines if animation is enabled and how should it play.

field.name: Animation
field.doc: Describes the sprite sheet grid used for animation, if animation is used.

class.doc: Implementation of SpriteImage that renders a vector path.

method.doc: Sets the vector path to render.

ctor.doc: Creates a new sprite vector path.
Creates a new sprite vector path.

struct.doc: Contains a resource that was imported from a file that contains multiple resources (for example an animation from an FBX file).

field.name: Name
field.doc: Unique name of the sub-resource.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Contents of the sub-resource.

class.doc: Module responsible for importing various asset types and converting them to types usable by the engine.

method.doc: Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. If file contains more than one resource only the primary resource is imported (for example an FBX a mesh would be imported, but animations ignored).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

param.name: importOptions
param.doc: (optional) Options for controlling the import. Caller must ensure import options actually match the type of the importer used for the file type.

param.name: UUID
param.doc: Specific UUID to assign to the resource. If not specified a randomly generated UUID will be assigned.

returns.doc: Imported resource.

method.doc: Same as import(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The resulting resource will be placed in the returned AsyncOp object when the import ends.

Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. This method returns all imported resources, which is relevant for files that can contain multiple resources (for example an FBX which may contain both a mesh and animations).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

param.name: importOptions
param.doc: (optional) Options for controlling the import. Caller must ensure import options actually match the type of the importer used for the file type.

returns.doc: A list of all imported resources. The primary resource is always the first returned resource.

method.doc: Same as importAll(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The returned AsyncOp will contain a list of the imported resources, after the import ends.
Checks if we can import a file with the specified extension.

param.name: extension
param.doc: The extension without the leading dot.

method.doc: Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. If file contains more than one resource only the primary resource is imported (for example an FBX a mesh would be imported, but animations ignored).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

param.name: importOptions
param.doc: (optional) Options for controlling the import. Caller must ensure import options actually match the type of the importer used for the file type.

returns.doc: Imported resource.

method.doc: Imports a resource at the specified location, and returns the loaded data. If file contains more than one resource only the primary resource is imported (for example an FBX a mesh would be imported, but animations ignored).

param.name: inputFilePath
param.doc: Pathname of the input file.

returns.doc: Imported resource.

method.doc: Same as import(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The resulting resource will be placed in the returned AsyncOp object when the import ends.
Same as import(), except it imports a resource without blocking the main thread. The resulting resource will be placed in the returned AsyncOp object when the import ends.

enum.doc: Controls what type of collision mesh should be imported during mesh import.

enumentry.doc: No collision mesh will be imported.
Normal triangle mesh will be imported.
A convex hull will be generated from the source mesh.

class.doc: Information about how to split an AnimationClip into multiple separate clips.
Contains import options you may use to control how is a mesh imported from some external format into engine format.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are meshes imported.

property.name: CpuCached
property.doc: Determines whether the texture data is also stored in CPU memory.

property.name: ImportNormals
property.doc: Determines should mesh normals be imported if available.

property.name: ImportTangents
property.doc: Determines should mesh tangents and bitangents be imported if available.

property.name: ImportBlendShapes
property.doc: Determines should mesh blend shapes be imported if available.

property.name: ImportSkin
property.doc: Determines should mesh skin data like bone weights, indices and bind poses be imported if available.

property.name: ImportAnimation
property.doc: Determines should animation clips be imported if available.

property.name: ReduceKeyFrames
property.doc: Enables or disables keyframe reduction. Keyframe reduction will reduce the number of key-frames in an animation clip by removing identical keyframes, and therefore reducing the size of the clip.

property.name: ImportRootMotion
property.doc: Enables or disables import of root motion curves. When enabled, any animation curves in imported animations affecting the root bone will be available through a set of separate curves in AnimationClip, and they won't be evaluated through normal animation process. Instead it is expected that the user evaluates the curves manually and applies them as required.

property.name: ImportScale
property.doc: Uniformly scales the imported mesh by the specified value.

property.name: CollisionMeshType
property.doc: Determines what type (if any) of collision mesh should be imported. If enabled the collision mesh will be available as a sub-resource returned by the importer (along with the normal mesh).

property.name: AnimationSplits
property.doc: Animation split infos that determine how will the source animation clip be split. If no splits are present the data will be imported as one clip, but if splits are present the data will be split according to the split infos. Split infos only affect the primary animation clip, other clips will not be split.

property.name: AnimationEvents
property.doc: Set of events that will be added to the animation clip, if animation import is enabled.

class.doc: Contains information about a single variation of a Shader. Each variation can have a separate set of #defines that control shader compilation.

method.doc: Returns the value of a signed integer parameter with the specified name. Returns 0 if the parameter cannot be found.
Returns the value of a unsigned integer parameter with the specified name. Returns 0 if the parameter cannot be found.
Returns the value of a float parameter with the specified name. Returns 0 if the parameter cannot be found.
Returns the value of a boolean parameter with the specified name. Returns false if the parameter cannot be found.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Sets the value of the parameter for the provided name. Any previous value for a parameter with the same name will be overwritten.
Removes a parameter with the specified name.
Checks if the variation has a parameter with the specified name.
Removes all parameters.

property.name: ParamNames
property.doc: Returns a list of names of all registered parameters.

struct.doc: Represents a single potential value of a shader variation parameter and optionally its name.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional human-readable name describing what this particular value represents.

field.name: Value
field.doc: Integer value of the parameter.

struct.doc: Represents a single shader variation parameter and a set of all possible values.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional human-readable name describing the variation parameter.

field.name: Identifier
field.doc: BSL identifier for the parameter.

field.name: IsInternal
field.doc: True if the parameter is for internal use by the renderer, and false if its intended to be set by the user.

field.name: Values
field.doc: A list of potential values this parameter can take on.

class.doc: Allows you to change and retrieve the active renderer. Active renderer will be used for rendering all objects in the following frame.

method.doc: Queues GPU command capture of the next frame, if a frame capture is set up.

enum.doc: Supported types of low-level shading languages.

enumentry.doc: Metal shading language.
High level shading language used by DirectX backend.
OpenGL shading language.
Metal shading language.
Helper entry that includes all languages.

class.doc: Serializable class that contains UUID <-> file path mapping for resources.

method.doc: Registers a new resource in the manifest.
Removes a resource from the manifest.
Attempts to find a resource with the provided UUID and outputs the path to the resource if found. Returns true if UUID was found, false otherwise.
Attempts to find a resource with the provided path and outputs the UUID to the resource if found. Returns true if path was found, false otherwise.
Checks if provided UUID exists in the manifest.
Checks if the provided path exists in the manifest.
Saves the resource manifest to the specified location.

param.name: manifest
param.doc: Manifest to save.

param.name: path
param.doc: Full pathname of the file to save the manifest in.

param.name: relativePath
param.doc: If not empty, all pathnames in the manifest will be stored as if relative to this path.

method.doc: Loads the resource manifest from the specified location.

param.name: path
param.doc: Full pathname of the file to load the manifest from.

param.name: relativePath
param.doc: If not empty, all loaded pathnames will have this path prepended.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty resource manifest. Provided name should be unique among manifests.

property.name: Name
property.doc: Returns an unique name of the resource manifest.

class.doc: Contains import options you may use to control how is a texture imported.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are textures imported.

property.name: Format
property.doc: Pixel format to import as.

property.name: GenerateMips
property.doc: Enables or disables mipmap generation for the texture.

property.name: MaxMip
property.doc: Maximum mip level to generate when generating mipmaps. If 0 then maximum amount of mip levels will be generated.

property.name: CpuCached
property.doc: Determines whether the texture data is also stored in main memory, available for fast CPU access.

property.name: SRgb
property.doc: Determines whether the texture data should be treated as if its in sRGB (gamma) space. Such texture will be converted by hardware to linear space before use on the GPU.

property.name: Cubemap
property.doc: Determines should the texture be imported as a cubemap. See setCubemapSource to choose how will the source texture be converted to a cubemap.

property.name: CubemapSourceType
property.doc: Determines how should the source texture be interpreted when generating a cubemap. Only relevant when is set to true.

enum.doc: A set of all languages that localized strings can be translated to. Loosely based on ISO 639-1 two letter language codes.

class.doc: Used for string localization. Stores strings and their translations in various languages.

method.doc: Checks does the string table contain the provided identifier.

param.name: identifier
param.doc: Identifier to look for.

returns.doc: True if the identifier exists in the table, false otherwise.

method.doc: Adds or modifies string translation for the specified language.
Returns a string translation for the specified language. Returns the identifier itself if one doesn't exist.
Removes the string described by identifier, from all languages.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty string table resource.

property.name: NumStrings
property.doc: Returns a total number of strings in the table.

property.name: Identifiers
property.doc: Returns all identifiers that the string table contains localized strings for.

class.doc: Manages string tables used for localizing text. Allows you to add and remove different tables and change the active language.

method.doc: Returns the string table with the specified id. If the table doesn't exist new one is created.

param.name: id
param.doc: Identifier of the string table.

returns.doc: String table with the specified identifier.

method.doc: Removes the string table with the specified id.

param.name: id
param.doc: Identifier of the string table.

method.doc: Registers a new string table or replaces an old one at the specified id.

param.name: id
param.doc: Identifier of the string table.

param.name: table
param.doc: New string table to assign to the specified identifier.

property.name: ActiveLanguage
property.doc: Determines the currently active language. Any newly created strings will use this value.

enum.doc: Planned usage for the mesh. These options usually affect performance and you should specify static if you don't plan on modifying the mesh often, otherwise specify dynamic.

enumentry.doc: Specify for a mesh that is not often updated from the CPU.
Specify for a mesh that is often updated from the CPU.
All mesh data will also be cached in CPU memory, making it available for fast read access from the CPU. Can be combined with other usage flags.

method.doc: Creates a deep copy of the material and returns the new object.
Assigns a float value to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a color to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.

Assigns a color gradient to the shader parameter with the specified name. The system will automatically evaluate the gradient with the passage of time and apply the evaluated value to the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 2D vector to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 3D vector to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 4D vector to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 3x3 matrix to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.
Assigns a 4x4 matrix to the shader parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index to assign the value to.

Returns a float value assigned with the parameter with the specified name. If a curve is assigned to this parameter, returns the curve value evaluated at time 0. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.

Returns a curve value assigned to the parameter with the specified name. If the parameter has a constant value bound instead of a curve then this method returns an empty curve. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.

Returns a color assigned with the parameter with the specified name. If a color gradient is assigned to this parameter, returns the gradient color evaluated at time 0. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.

Returns a color gradient assigned with the parameter with the specified name. If the parameter has a constant value bound instead of a gradient then this method returns an empty gradient. Use getBoundParamType() to determine the type of the parameter. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 2D vector assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 3D vector assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 4D vector assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 3x3 matrix assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Returns a 4x4 matrix assigned with the parameter with the specified name. Optionally if the parameter is an array you may provide an array index you which to retrieve.
Checks does the data parameter with the specified name currently contains animated data. This could be an animation curve or a color gradient.

ctor.doc: Creates a new empty material.
Creates a new material with the specified shader.

property.name: Shader
property.doc: Sets a shader that will be used by the material. Material will be initialized using all compatible techniques from the shader. Shader must be set before doing any other operations with the material.

property.name: Variation
property.doc: Set of parameters that determine which subset of techniques in the assigned shader should be used. Only the techniques that have the provided parameters with the provided values will match. This will control which technique is considered the default technique and which subset of techniques are searched during a call to findTechnique().

class.doc: Provides global physics settings, factory methods for physics objects and scene queries.

method.doc: Enables or disables collision between two layers. Each physics object can be assigned a specific layer, and here you can determine which layers can interact with each other.
Checks if two collision layers are allowed to interact.

property.name: IsUpdateInProgress
property.doc: Checks is the physics simulation update currently in progress.

class.doc: Physical representation of a scene, allowing creation of new physical objects in the scene and queries against those objects. Objects created in different scenes cannot physically interact with eachother.

method.doc: Casts a ray into the scene and returns the closest found hit, if any.

param.name: ray
param.doc: Ray to cast into the scene.

param.name: hit
param.doc: Information recorded about a hit. Only valid if method returns true.

param.name: layer
param.doc: Layers to consider for the query. This allows you to ignore certain groups of objects.

param.name: max
param.doc: Maximum distance at which to perform the query. Hits past this distance will not be detected.

returns.doc: True if something was hit, false otherwise.

param.name: origin
param.doc: Origin of the ray to cast into the scene.

param.name: unitDir
param.doc: Unit direction of the ray to cast into the scene.

param.name: hit
param.doc: Information recorded about a hit. Only valid if method returns true.

param.name: layer
param.doc: Layers to consider for the query. This allows you to ignore certain groups of objects.

param.name: max
param.doc: Maximum distance at which to perform the query. Hits past this distance will not be detected.

returns.doc: True if something was hit, false otherwise.

method.doc: Adds a new physics region. Certain physics options require you to set up regions in which physics objects are allowed to be in, and objects outside of these regions will not be handled by physics. You do not need to set up these regions by default.
Removes a physics region.
Removes all physics regions.

property.name: Gravity
property.doc: Determines the global gravity value for all objects in the scene.

class.doc: Material that controls how two physical objects interact with each other. Materials of both objects are used during their interaction and their combined values are used.

ctor.doc: Creates a new physics material.

param.name: staticFriction
param.doc: Controls friction when two in-contact objects are not moving lateral to each other (for example how difficult is to get an object moving from a static state while it is in contact other object(s)).

param.name: dynamicFriction
param.doc: Sets dynamic friction of the material. Controls friction when two in-contact objects are moving lateral to each other (for example how quickly does an object slow down when sliding along another object).

param.name: restitution
param.doc: Controls "bounciness" of an object during a collision. Value of 1 means the collision is elastic, and value of 0 means the value is inelastic. Must be in [0, 1] range.

property.name: StaticFriction
property.doc: Controls friction when two in-contact objects are not moving lateral to each other (for example how difficult it is to get an object moving from a static state while it is in contact with other object(s)).

property.name: DynamicFriction
property.doc: Controls friction when two in-contact objects are moving lateral to each other (for example how quickly does an object slow down when sliding along another object).

property.name: Restitution
property.doc: Controls "bounciness" of an object during a collision. Value of 1 means the collision is elastic, and value of 0 means the value is inelastic. Must be in [0, 1] range.

enum.doc: Flags that can be used to control resource loading.

enumentry.doc: No flags.
If enabled all resources referenced by the root resource will be loaded as well.

If enabled the resource system will keep an internal reference to the resource so it doesn't get destroyed when it goes out of scope. You can call Resources::release() to release the internal reference. Each call to load will create a new internal reference and therefore must be followed by the same number of release calls. If dependencies are being loaded, they will not have internal references created regardless of this parameter.

Determines if the loaded resource keeps original data loaded. Sometime resources will process loaded data and discard the original (e.g. uncompressing audio on load). This flag can prevent the resource from discarding the original data. The original data might be required for saving the resource (via Resources::save), but will use up extra memory. Normally you want to keep this enabled if you plan on saving the resource to disk.
Default set of flags used for resource loading.

class.doc: Manager for dealing with all engine resources. It allows you to save new resources and load existing ones.

method.doc: Loads the resource from a given path. Returns an empty handle if resource can't be loaded. Resource is loaded synchronously.

param.name: filePath
param.doc: File path to the resource to load. This can be absolute or relative to the working folder.

param.name: loadFlags
param.doc: Flags used to control the load process.

method.doc: Loads the resource asynchronously. Initially returned resource handle will be invalid until resource loading is done. Use ResourceHandle<T>::isLoaded to check if resource has been loaded, or ResourceHandle<T>::blockUntilLoaded to wait until load completes.

param.name: filePath
param.doc: Full pathname of the file.

param.name: loadFlags
param.doc: Flags used to control the load process.

method.doc: Loads the resource with the given UUID. Returns an empty handle if resource can't be loaded.

param.name: uuid
param.doc: UUID of the resource to load.

param.name: async
param.doc: If true resource will be loaded asynchronously. Handle to non-loaded resource will be returned immediately while loading will continue in the background.

param.name: loadFlags
param.doc: Flags used to control the load process.

method.doc: Releases an internal reference to the resource held by the resources system. This allows the resource to be unloaded when it goes out of scope, if the resource was loaded with parameter. Alternatively you can also skip manually calling release() and call unloadAllUnused() which will unload all resources that do not have any external references, but you lose the fine grained control of what will be unloaded.

param.name: resource
param.doc: Handle of the resource to release.

method.doc: Finds all resources that aren't being referenced outside of the resources system and unloads them.
Forces unload of all resources, whether they are being used or not.
Saves the resource at the specified location.

param.name: resource
param.doc: Handle to the resource.

param.name: filePath
param.doc: Full pathname of the file to save as.

param.name: overwrite
param.doc: If true, any existing resource at the specified location will be overwritten.

param.name: compress
param.doc: Should the resource be compressed before saving. Some resources have data that is already compressed and this option will be ignored for such resources.

method.doc: Saves an existing resource to its previous location.

param.name: resource
param.doc: Handle to the resource.

param.name: compress
param.doc: Should the resource be compressed before saving. Some resources have data that is already compressed and this option will be ignored for such resources.

method.doc: Returns a list of dependencies from the resources at the specified path. Resource will not be loaded or parsed, but instead the saved list of dependencies will be read from the file and returned.

param.name: filePath
param.doc: Full path to the resource to get dependencies for.

returns.doc: List of dependencies represented as UUIDs.

method.doc: Checks is the resource with the specified UUID loaded.

param.name: uuid
param.doc: UUID of the resource to check.

param.name: checkInProgress
param.doc: Should this method also check resources that are in progress of being asynchronously loaded.

returns.doc: True if loaded or loading in progress, false otherwise.

method.doc: Returns the loading progress of a resource that's being asynchronously loaded.

param.name: resource
param.doc: Resource whose load progress to check.

param.name: includeDependencies
param.doc: If false the progress will reflect the load progress only for this inidividual resource. If true the progress will reflect load progress of this resource and all of its dependencies.

returns.doc: Load progress in range [0, 1].

method.doc: Allows you to set a resource manifest containing UUID <-> file path mapping that is used when resolving resource references.
Unregisters a resource manifest previously registered with registerResourceManifest().
Allows you to retrieve resource manifest containing UUID <-> file path mapping that is used when resolving resource references.
Attempts to retrieve file path from the provided UUID. Returns true if successful, false otherwise.
Attempts to retrieve UUID from the provided file path. Returns true if successful, false otherwise.

event.doc: Called when the resource has been successfully loaded.
Called when the resource has been destroyed. Provides UUID of the destroyed resource.
Called when the internal resource the handle is pointing to has changed.

struct.doc: Kerning pair representing larger or smaller offset between a specific pair of characters.
Describes a single character in a font of a specific size.

field.name: CharId
field.doc: Character ID, corresponding to a Unicode key.

field.name: Page
field.doc: Index of the texture the character is located on.

field.name: UvX
field.doc: Texture coordinates of the character in the page texture.

field.name: UvY
field.doc: Texture coordinates of the character in the page texture.

field.name: UvWidth
field.doc: Width/height of the character in texture coordinates.

field.name: UvHeight
field.doc: Width/height of the character in texture coordinates.

field.name: Width
field.doc: Width/height of the character in pixels.

field.name: Height
field.doc: Width/height of the character in pixels.

field.name: XOffset
field.doc: Offset for the visible portion of the character in pixels.

field.name: YOffset
field.doc: Offset for the visible portion of the character in pixels.

field.name: XAdvance
field.doc: Determines how much to advance the pen after writing this character, in pixels.

field.name: YAdvance
field.doc: Determines how much to advance the pen after writing this character, in pixels.

field.name: KerningPairs
field.doc: Pairs that determine if certain character pairs should be closer or father together. for example "AV" combination.

class.doc: Contains information about font characters rendered into one or multiple bitmaps, for specific font size.

method.doc: Returns a character description for the character with the specified Unicode key.

property.name: Size
property.doc: Font size for which the bitmaps are rendered.

property.name: BaselineOffset
property.doc: Y offset to the baseline on which the characters are placed, in pixels.

property.name: LineHeight
property.doc: Height of a single line of the font, in pixels.

property.name: MissingGlyph
property.doc: Character to use when data for a character is missing.

property.name: SpaceWidth
property.doc: Width of a space in pixels.

property.name: TexturePages
property.doc: Textures in which the character's pixels are stored.

struct.doc: Information about a Font.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Optional name of the font. Used primarily for easier debugging.

field.name: DPI
field.doc: Determines dots per inch scale that will be used when rendering the characters.

field.name: RenderMode
field.doc: Determines the render mode used for rendering the characters into a bitmap.

class.doc: Font resource containing data about textual characters and how to render text. Contains one or multiple font bitmaps, each for a specific size.

method.doc: Returns font bitmap information for a specific font size.

param.name: size
param.doc: Size of the font in points.

returns.doc: Bitmap object if it exists, false otherwise.

method.doc: Finds the available font bitmap size closest to the provided size.

param.name: size
param.doc: Size of the bitmap in points.

returns.doc: Nearest available bitmap size.

struct.doc: Represents a range of character code.

class.doc: Import options that allow you to control how is a font imported.

ctor.doc: Creates a new import options object that allows you to customize how are fonts imported.

property.name: FontSizes
property.doc: Determines font sizes that are to be imported. Sizes are in points.

property.name: CharIndexRanges
property.doc: Determines character index ranges to import. Ranges are defined as unicode numbers.

property.name: Dpi
property.doc: Determines dots per inch scale that will be used when rendering the characters.

property.name: RenderMode
property.doc: Determines the render mode used for rendering the characters into a bitmap.

property.name: Bold
property.doc: Determines whether the bold font style should be used when rendering.

property.name: Italic
property.doc: Determines whether the italic font style should be used when rendering.

enum.doc: Contains a list of all icons that can be retrieved through the StockIcons module.
Determines how is text vertically aligned in a GUI element.
Possible positions used for positioning content image within a GUI element.

struct.doc: Structure describing contents of a GUITexture element.

field.name: Image
field.doc: Image to display. If this is null then the image specified by the style will be used.

field.name: ScaleMode
field.doc: Scale mode to use when sizing the texture.

field.name: IsTransparent
field.doc: Determines should the texture be rendered with transparency active.

class.doc: GUI element style that determines the look of a GUI element, as well as the element's default layout options. Different looks can be provided for different element states.

method.doc: Registers a new sub-style that is used by complex GUI elements that contain one or multiple sub-elements.

param.name: guiType
param.doc: Name of the sub-element this style is to be used for. This depends on GUI element the style is applied to.

param.name: styleName
param.doc: Name of the style in GUI skin to use for the sub-element.

property.name: Font
property.doc: Font to use for all text within the GUI element.

property.name: FontSize
property.doc: Font size to use for all text within the GUI element.

property.name: TextHorzAlign
property.doc: Horizontal alignment of text within the GUI element.

property.name: TextVertAlign
property.doc: Vertical alignment of text within the GUI element.

property.name: ImagePosition
property.doc: Position of content image relative to text.

property.name: WordWrap
property.doc: Should the text word wrap if it doesn't fit.

property.name: Normal
property.doc: Style used when the element doesn't have focus nor is the user interacting with the element. Used when the element is in the 'off' state.

property.name: Hover
property.doc: Style used when the user is hovering the pointer over the element, while the element doesn't have focus. Used when the element is in the 'off' state.

property.name: Active
property.doc: Style used when the user is actively interacting with the element. Used when the element is in the 'off' state.

property.name: Focused
property.doc: Style used when the element has focus but the pointer is not hovering over the element. Used when the element is in the 'off' state.

property.name: FocusedHover
property.doc: Style used when the element has focus and the pointer is hovering over the element. Used when the element is in the 'off' state.

property.name: NormalOn
property.doc: Same as GUIElementStyle::normal, except it's used when element is in the 'on' state.

property.name: HoverOn
property.doc: Same as GUIElementStyle::hover, except it's used when element is in the 'on' state.

property.name: ActiveOn
property.doc: Same as GUIElementStyle::active, except it's used when element is in the 'on' state.

property.name: FocusedOn
property.doc: Same as GUIElementStyle::focused, except it's used when element is in the 'on' state.

property.name: FocusedHoverOn
property.doc: Same as GUIElementStyle::focusedHover, except it's used when element is in the 'on' state.

property.name: Border
property.doc: Determines how the element is scaled (using the typical Scale9Grid approach).

property.name: Margins
property.doc: Determines offset from the background graphics to the content. Input uses bounds offset by this value.

property.name: ContentOffset
property.doc: Additional offset to the content, that doesn't effect the bounds. Applied on top of the margins offsets.

property.name: Padding
property.doc: Determines extra distance between this and other elements in a layout.

property.name: Width
property.doc: Wanted width of the GUI element in pixels. Only used if fixedWidth is enabled.

property.name: Height
property.doc: Wanted height of the GUI element in pixels. Only used if fixedHeight is enabled.

property.name: MinWidth
property.doc: Minimum width allowed for the GUI element. Used by the layout only when exact width is not specified.

property.name: MaxWidth
property.doc: Maximum width allowed for the GUI element. Used by the layout only when exact width is not specified.

property.name: MinHeight
property.doc: Minimum height allowed for the GUI element. Used by the layout only when exact height is not specified.

property.name: MaxHeight
property.doc: Maximum height allowed for the GUI element. Used by the layout only when exact height is not specified.

property.name: FixedWidth
property.doc: Determines should the layout resize the element depending on available size. If true no resizing will be done.

property.name: FixedHeight
property.doc: Determines should the layout resize the element depending on available size. If true no resizing will be done.

class.doc: Holds a set of styles that control how are GUI element types positioned and displayed in the GUI. Each element type can be assigned a specific style.

method.doc: Checks if the style with the specified name exists.
Returns a style for the specified GUI element type.
Sets a style for the specified GUI element type.
Removes a style for the specified GUI element type.

ctor.doc: Creates an empty GUI skin and returns a handle to it.

property.name: StyleNames
property.doc: Returns names of all styles registered on this skin.

enum.doc: Types of builtin shaders that are always available.

enumentry.doc: Physically based shader used for opaque 3D geometry.
Physically based shader used for transparent 3D geometry.
Special shader used for rendering particles without any lighting, with support for transparency.
Special shader used for rendering particles with lighting using the forward rendering pipeline (supports transparency).
Special shader used for rendering particles with lighting using the deferred rendering pipeline (no support for transparency).
Special shader used for rendering decals that project onto other geometry.

struct.doc: Controls GUI element layout options, possibly by overriding the default options specified in GUI element style. These options control GUI element placement and size in a GUI layout.

enum.doc: Type of GUI element states.

enumentry.doc: Same as Hover, if the element is also in the "on" state.
Normal state when element is not being iteracted with.
Same as Normal, if the element is also in the "on" state.
State when pointer is hovering over the element.
State when element is being clicked.
State when the element has input focus and pointer is hovering over the element.
State when the element has input focus and pointer is not hovering over the element.
Same as Active, if the element is also in the "on" state.
Same as Focused, if the element is also in the "on" state.
Same as FocusedHover, if the element is also in the "on" state.

struct.doc: Holds data used for displaying content in a GUIElement. Content can consist of a string, image, a tooltip or none of those.

ctor.doc: Constructs an empty content.
Constructs content with just a string.
Constructs content with a string and a tooltip.
Constructs content with just an image.
Constructs content with an image and a tooltip.
Constructs content with a string and an image.
Constructs content with a string, an image and a tooltip.

class.doc: GUI button that can be clicked. Has normal, hover and active states with an optional label.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: contents
param.doc: Structure describing the contents of the GUI element to create.

param.name: styleClass
param.doc: Style class that will be used for determining GUI element visuals from the current style sheet. If no class is provided, default style is determined based on GUI element type.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

param.name: options

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

ctor.doc: Creates a new GUI element.

param.name: options
param.doc: Additional options that control GUI element size and position. This will override options set in the style sheet.

method.doc: Changes the active image. If the provided image is null then the image specified by the style will be used.

class.doc: Object that allows you to group multiple GUI toggle buttons. Only one button among the grouped ones can be active.

ctor.doc: Creates a toggle group that you may provide to GUIToggleable upon construction. Toggles sharing the same group will only have a single element active at a time.

param.name: allowAllOff
param.doc: If true all of the toggle buttons can be turned off, if false one will always be turned on.

struct.doc: Structure describing contents of a GUIToggle element.

class.doc: GUI element representing a toggleable button.

property.name: IsToggled
property.doc: Checks or unchecks the toggle.

event.doc: Triggered whenever the button is toggled on or off.

struct.doc: Contains information about a single shader parameter.

field.name: Name
field.doc: Name of the parameter variable.

field.name: Identifier
field.doc: Variable identifier of the parameter.

field.name: Type
field.doc: Data type of the parameter.

field.name: Flags
field.doc: Flags used to further describe the parameter.
